review_text:
Our family has been part of the Clementi Woods Kindergarten (CWK) community for several years, and we couldn’t be more grateful for the experience.

From day one, CWK has felt like a second home — a place where our child has been cared for and encouraged.  Every child is welcomed by name, from the morning greetings at the gate to the cheerful assembly and into the classroom. It’s something that truly touched us — knowing that not just our child, but every student is known personally by the staff.

The teachers, many of whom have been with the school for over a decade, bring not only experience but a deep love for what they do. They take time to understand each child, helping them grow in confidence, independence, and character.

What sets CWK apart is its strong foundation in faith and values. The principles of love, care, sharing, giving, and growing aren’t just taught — they’re lived out daily in the classroom and playground. The admin team, led by the principal, is warm, responsive, and always open to feedback. We’ve felt heard and supported every step of the way.

One of our child’s favourite parts of the day has been the morning assembly — filled with praise and worship songs, laughter, and energy. It’s a joyful start that sets the tone for learning and connection.

CWK may not have all the bells and whistles of a commercial preschool, but it offers something far more valuable: a safe, consistent, and faith grounded environment where children thrive.

We’re so thankful for the strong foundation it’s given our child — and for the community we’ve found here.

A fairly good kindergarten with kind and helpful staff. The admin team is really good and accommodating.

However, the start of each day at CWK is overstimulating for a preschooler as loud music will be blasted at assembly - I supposed the school wants to hype up the entire atmosphere for kids but a more calming start might be an easier transition for kids.

Some teaching staff have been around for more than 15 years so some of their methods, though well-intentioned, might be less attuned to the needs of current generation of kids and borders on being disempowering for children who need a little more support. There is a lot of focus on traditional teaching methods like worksheets and less hands-on activities that promotes experiential learning and creative thinking.

School bus driver is a little eccentric and would pick a bone with parents who enter the school compound earlier (understandably, cars can only enter at 2.20pm due to limited space available) - even if permission has been sought to enter the school (happened to me twice, and I’ve witnessed this happen to other parents several times too).

I will recommend this school for a faith-based environment, super nice/easy to work with admin team (principal, admin, etc). However, if you’re looking for teachers who are nurturing, patient, supportive, and updated in their teaching methods, it might be less than desirable. The school is big on conformity and very unsupportive of independent thinking.

We have switched our child from this school to another school and found that we really prefer a quieter/ calming start to the day and we find it more empowering for our preschooler to be in the company of teachers who understand the need for hands-on learning, are patient and more nurturing. We find our child now more confident and less scared to express her views in class. It’s regretful that our child did not have a positive experience with her teachers at CWK as she often talks about her fear of being scolded. This has a lasting impact on her that we are still trying to work on how. It’s very regretful and one of the poorest choices I have made.

This is a personal and honest view from my end, with due respect given to the school and their staff. Written with the sole intent for other parents to understand the environment before they dive in as quite a lot of energy, time and money will be involved if you choose to enrol in any school of your choice.

This is the best kindergarten you can send your child to. All my four children attend this kindergarten.

It is not expensive. They have a holistic program that expose your child to various subjects.

They teach your children life skills , math and also Chinese and handicraft.

The Chinese teacher speaks to your child only in mandarin, which helps stem learn the language.

The children love going to school and it gives them such a good impression of school, starting them on the right track to  learning.

If you are interested in sending your child here, you need to queue up early to register because it is extremely popular.

Furthermore, the uniform looks great  😃

I am so grateful for what CWK has done for my kids. They acquired discipline, maintained healthy habits and happiness, while excelled academically. The teachers are loving and supportive. I can't ask for more.

The pastoral aspect is very good. During HBL, we appreciated the effort to create engaging in-house videos and worksheets/ workbooks.

Edited:
As many private kindergartens no longer operate because of the changing landscape and costing, I hope to express our family's gratitude. CWK has taught my child self-discipline, a desire to do one's best and to behave with integrity. Not that it really matters, but of the 6 children who went into her primary school, 4 are in the stretch classes.

For us, it was not so much the quantity of content but the nurturing of the character that made the difference. Reflective learning , being metacognitive, has made her purposeful.

My child is proud to say she was a CWK kid and 5 years after K2, she tells me she wishes she could re-live her Kindy years.

The teachers there are so nice and gentle with the kids that it makes my kids want to be able to open up to them. So far no negative feedback from my kids which is comforting for me as a mother, knowing my kids are in good hands. They come home learning something new everyday. On top of that, the centre is also very spacious and well kept. Would definitely recommend this centre to others 👍🏽😊

We’ve had a positive experience with Skool4Kidz Preschool @ Tengah Parc Glen. The teachers are caring, patient, and supportive, and we’ve seen clear improvement in our child’s confidence, communication, and overall development.

Overall, a reliable and supportive preschool with a dedicated teaching team.
Love the centre's facilities. Extremely spacious for children to move around. Kids enjoy going to school.
The centre is very spacious. All the teachers are very friendly. Our children enjoy the school every day.
My daughter totally love to go school on her own. Greatly improved Chinese language and english.

This children care very good  , teachers is friendly, very Nice and take care very good of our kids. My baby she very happy when joining with them can learn a lot of good thing. We very happy thankful to all teachers.

I am thrilled to share my positive experience with ICS School, where my child has been a student for the past two years. The school's commitment to biblical teachings is truly praiseworthy. Students actively engage in Bible memorization, prayer, and weekly worship, fostering a profound connection with God from an early age.

The teachers at ICS School deserve special commendation. Their warmth and patience stand out, empowering students to build confidence and cultivate politeness. The well-maintained teacher-to-student ratio ensures personalized attention, contributing to a nurturing learning environment.

Beyond academics, I appreciate the school's efforts in organizing various engaging activities that strengthen the connection between parents, teachers, and students. These initiatives create a vibrant community and enhance the overall educational experience.

In summary, I am grateful for the positive impact ICS School has had on my child's spiritual and academic growth. The dedication to character development, coupled with a supportive community, makes ICS School an exceptional choice for families seeking a well-rounded education.

I am a parent of two children, one in middle school and another in elementary school, both attending ICS (International Community School). Moving from India to Singapore, we were searching for a school that would be a blessing for our family, and we are incredibly grateful to have found ICS.

The school follows the American curriculum, and we couldn't be happier with how it has nurtured our children not only academically but also as individuals with a serving heart. The teachers at ICS are exceptional, creating countless opportunities for experiential learning and fostering critical thinking, creativity, communication skills, and teamwork.

As a family, we have been impressed by the school's commitment to providing an international exposure and academic growth. The holistic educational experience offered by ICS has truly exceeded our expectations.

We wholeheartedly recommend ICS to all families seeking a school that not only offers a top-notch education but also prioritizes the overall development of students. Moving to Singapore and finding this school has been a true blessing for us.

A big thank you to the entire ICS community for providing our children with an enriching and supportive learning environment!

Out of all the international schools that I've visited, ICS was the best suited for my child. It had the right balance of academic rigor, pastoral care and just great vibes from everyone that we met. My child continues to flourish because of the genuine and intentional interest and care from teachers and staff.

The most genuine people here, a very safe place and the children feel loved. It’s where all the children are seen and known—really cared for. The staff knows the children on a first name basis. Thank you ICS!!

We are so thankful for our time at ICS. It has been such a blessing to be part of a school that is truly rooted in Christian values. The education my kid received was not only academically solid but also grounded in faith, character, and love for others.

What stood out most to us was the dedication of the teachers and staff. They pour their hearts into the students, and that heart of service has made a lasting impression on our family. The ICS community is small, warm, and close-knit, and my kids made wonderful friendships that we know will stay with them for a life time. We are especially grateful for the wonderful Parent Teacher Fellowship and the Moms in Prayer team, who faithfully support the school and community with love, encouragement, and prayer. Their presence is a beautiful reflection of the Christ-centered spirit that makes ICS feel like a second home.

Highly recommend this school to any family looking for strong academics, caring community, and a Christ-centered education.
Attended ACJC's traditional biennial funfair. Such an exciting event! Kudos to all the students and teachers who made the event so grand.

Not only does this school love sucking out money from its students to allegedly maintain facilities that 1. students NEVER get to use and 2. NEVER become cleaner or better in condition, they also enjoy developing bladder diseases in their poor students.

Teachers, especially the discipline master, ridicule and punish, sometimes even forbid, students from simply using the toilet. A 5 minute trip to the toilet to empty one’s bladder from holding it in for hours is seen as an act of sin, a crime being committed by this school that praises itself for being so loving with the grace of God.

During chapel, assembly, or school events where students are gathered in the hall, using to the toilet is seen as a threat, a crime. Students will either be held captive in another room where they will not even be able to see what is going on in the main hall to appreciate the event, will be told to take the route that is much longer to come back to the hall (which makes absolutely no sense as one would have to walk past much more people if their reason is disturbing the course of event), or simply, told no and scolded for “not being able to hold it in”.

Teachers say to students in a humiliating, condescending tone that if they cannot hold their bladder for more than a few hours, they should “get it checked”. This is either implying that students should not stay hydrated or using the toilet is undesirable, when it is part and parcel of how the normal human body works. Over time, with students not being able to use the restroom when they urgently need it, kidney and bladder diseases may be manifested. Urinary Tract Infections are amongst one of the many diseases students would have a much higher chance of having after holding in their urine for extended periods of time just to adhere to some school expectations. Not only that, it is simply extremely uncomfortable sitting still with a full bladder.

I sincerely hope the school will be more considerate of students’ health and needs and be more understanding.

I went to ACJC this morning at 11:30am as I wanted to purchase a swimming paddle for my child. At the entrance, the security guard Mr. Liao asked me to register and requested for my IC. I simply asked him whether it was necessary to provide my IC, but instead of explaining politely, he responded in a rude manner and told me not to “talk too much,” threatening not to let me in and even challenging me to complain to the authorities.

I believe such an attitude is not appropriate, especially within a school environment that should set an example for students in communication and respect. If students were to overhear such an exchange, what impression would they be left with?

I sincerely hope the school can look into this matter and ensure better communication and professionalism from the staff. Your improvement will be very much appreciated.

Wonderful Junior College providing a holistic education. Tremendously benefitted from my experience here at ACJC in 2003-2004, with lifelong friendships and bonds still going strong 20+ years later. Blessed to be part of the ACSian family, with teachers and even our former Principal being ever supportive of our individual endeavours. The sports facilities were fantastic too from the track to the swimming pool and gym, I credit a lot of my sporting journey from this too. Thank you, ACJC! 🙏🏻

The location is strategic, as it sits between 2 mrt stations, and surrounded by many eateries and shopping malls. The sport complex is big, there's football field, running track, swimming pools, as well as badminton court.

Bunch of ridiculous students at cck interchange always not queuing up for 190, while the senior citizens all could. And just going up the bus without waiting for the queue to clear. Smart but lack of common sense.
A big shoutout to Mr Steve Poh, Mdm Shasila & Mr Lee Wei Chai who helped me survive in PJC

Shoutout to Mr Tan KG, #1 PE Teacher that helped me get 200 points for IPPT and Top in Inter Class Games. I have benefited greatly from his teaching and I have flourished, from a cocoon to a butterfly. Looking forward to becoming a PE Teacher in the future and nurture the next batch of CHAMPIONS!!! JP ALWAYS STRIVES AS ONE #INFINITEGROWTHBABY
Shoutout to Mr Subash Ramiah wansuey math teacher #goatedasf. He explained everything in life using Mathematics. I will want to become a Math Prof in the future 😍😍😍😍
Very caring teachers that go to the mile to educate. Learn to be a leader. Teachers are very experienced with long service years

Beautiful big campus. Can be quite a walk to the various schools. The canteen near the health sciences zone is very spacious, and quite a few international cuisines on offer. Lucky staff and students.
The staff and students at the optometry dept were all polite and professional. The Facility looked like a very good place to groom future optometrist.

​Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) offers a highly practical and industry-focused education. The curriculum is regularly updated with input from industry partners, and lecturers, often with professional experience, provide a realistic and engaging learning environment. The focus on project-based learning encourages critical thinking and problem-solving, which I found to be a more effective method of learning than traditional memorization.

​While the polytechnic's modular system can be demanding, with a constant cycle of assignments and projects, it effectively prepares students for the pressures of both higher education and the workplace. The campus life is equally vibrant, with a wide array of CCAs, excellent facilities, and diverse food options.

​Overall, NP provides a well-rounded experience, blending academic rigor with a rich student life. It's a great choice for students who thrive in a hands-on environment and are seeking to build a strong foundation for their future careers.

It sounds like you're planning a trip to Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) Block 23! Here's a quick rundown to help you get there:

1. Take the MRT to Clementi Station.
2. At Clementi Station, exit towards Bus Interchange.
3. Board Bus 154 from Clementi Bus Interchange.
4. Get off at Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) stop.

You can use Google Maps or a transit app like Grab or Moovit for the most up-to-date bus schedules and routes. Have a smooth ride to your company event! 🚍🎉..

Ngee Ann Polytechnic has this warm, almost “campus village” vibe. The greenery is everywhere — huge trees, long slopes, and shaded areas that make the place feel peaceful even when there are a lot of students around. NP is known for being very student-focused, and you can actually feel that when walking through the school. There are tons of small places to sit down and study, from open-air benches to indoor lounges.

The campus spirit is strong. A lot of students seem genuinely happy to be there — you’ll see groups practising dance, people playing guitar, students sitting around chatting after lessons, and clubs actively doing their thing. The Co-Curricular culture is particularly strong; NP is one of the polys where the arts and student life are very vibrant.

What I liked:

Extremely beautiful surroundings; feels like studying in a nature park sometimes.

Many programmes are well-known and respected, especially mass comm and business.

Good social vibes — friendly atmosphere and active clubs everywhere.

Lots of cozy corners to study, hang out, or relax between classes.

What could be better:

The slopes are legendary — going up and down the hills every day becomes a workout.

Classrooms and older blocks are slightly dated in some areas.

Some canteens fill up very quickly, especially during lunch hours.

Overall:
NP feels like a warm, welcoming campus with plenty of life. If you like greenery, community feeling, and a comfortable environment, this is a great place.

I'm here waiting for my friend and my first time here. So natural greeny, relaxing and peaceful. Facilities here clean, stable and comfy ambience. Love it here. Would recommend to all friend and family.

Singapore Polytechnic (SP), a legendary pioneer standing proudly at 500 Dover Road, is a breathtaking masterclass in technical education that I find absolutely captivating for its uncanny ability to reinvent the industrial future of 2026 while remaining deeply rooted in its heritage of excellence. Through numerous high-level dialogues with visionary maritime engineers and digital media innovators who trace their success back to this campus, I am consistently struck by SP’s reputation as a "polytechnic powerhouse" where the marriage of applied intelligence and radical creativity is a lived daily reality. Based on glowing testimonials from close associates who have thrived within its specialized clusters, it is evident that the institution operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where students are empowered to master complex aeronautics or sustainability systems under the guidance of mentors who are genuine titans of industry. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, viewing this Dover landmark not merely as a school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in Singapore’s engineering, life sciences, and business sectors. Reflecting on these authentic accounts from peers who have spearheaded national-level projects, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of technological audacity and civic grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "SP Way," which my acquaintances describe as a unique pedagogical energy that balances rigorous hands-on training with the collaborative soft skills required for global leadership. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring innovator as a vital architect of our future smart-nation infrastructure. It is heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the polytechnic’s commitment to lifelong learning and industry-integrated projects provides a distinct competitive edge that is respected across the international labor market. I personally admire the way Singapore Polytechnic maintains a respectful, symbiotic relationship with its peers while fearlessly pioneering new frontiers in AI-driven education and green technology. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and professional transformation. Those who have navigated its rigorous curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful ambition is meticulously refined into elite technical expertise within a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. Ultimately, aligning one’s career trajectory with Singapore Polytechnic is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to lead the 21st-century technological transition with confidence and integrity.
Amazing! It is great place for the student. I visited for a mentoring session and it was a great experience.

Singapore Polytechnic honestly feels like its own ecosystem. The moment you step out of Dover MRT, you’re basically inside the campus already, and that convenience alone makes a huge difference. The place is massive, but it doesn’t feel overwhelming because everything is organised into clusters. Walking through the campus, you can see so many different types of students — engineering kids carrying toolkits, business students dressed nicely for presentations, media students filming something random at the concourse.

The school has a very “industry-ready” vibe. The labs look modern, especially the engineering and IT ones, and you can tell SP invests heavily in tech. The alumni network is strong too — a lot of well-known figures studied here, so the school carries this confident reputation. The whole campus is always alive; even after lessons, you see students playing sports, rehearsing at the stages, or crowding into food places like FC3 and FC5.

What I liked:

Super clean and modern environment — nothing feels rundown.

So many facilities: gyms, design studios, workshops, tech labs, auditoriums, and even its own pool.

Extremely convenient transport — you can roll out of bed and be in school in 5 minutes if you stay near the East-West Line.

The culture feels mature, almost like mini-university life.

What could be better:

Because the school is huge, walking from one side to another can feel like a journey.

Popular food places get packed extremely fast during lunch.

Some of the older blocks are showing age, but still in good condition.

Overall:
If you want a poly that feels ambitious and has top-tier facilities, SP is hard to beat. It feels like a place where people genuinely get things done.

Easy place to access as its near Dover mrt. May need a student pass to get thru the gantry. I was there to attend a public workshop and so the helpful security assisted me. Many students I met to ask for directions were very helpful. There are a few Food Courts (air-conditioning and non air-conditioning) plus varieties of food at reasonable price. As the venue is huge, one need to walk (take it as part of exercise). O
Present for graduation day a well known polytechnic in Singapore

After Moving to New Location: 4

Prior to Moving: 3.5
I would say a 3.5. The campus is smaller than what it appears to be online but it is still nice and cosy nonetheless. With the right lighting, it can be good a good place to take photos!

Since it is a small campus, the facilities aren't exactly what I'm used to but it is still sufficient.

Still the school will be moving to a different location in 2022 if I'm not mistaken. Hence, hopefully the facilities will be better. 👍🏼

Full of students who can't speak English. Lecturers cater to their use of ChatGPT and cause the rest of us problems with our grades. Not sure what they're teaching them their preparatory English course, probably how to be a burden to the rest of their group mates in classes

Before coming I was looking at their reviews but they didn’t have any comprehensive ones that could facilitate my decision to enrol here — So now that I’ve graduated I thought of leaving an honest one that not only speaks of my experience but considers other perspectives too. Overall, would I recommend? Yes. I had a good experience.

The best part would be the lecturers: They were extremely experienced, (ex ceos, ex finance ex journalist etc) passionate and skilful (with a minor exception of a few not so great ones, but not the not so great ones don’t usually stay for a long time). Most of the lecturers made me feel so safe, laugh a lot and learn a lot. They truly did a great job hiring lecturers! I can’t express how much I loved them❤️ I felt so bittersweet with the end of each  trimester! The college operates in a trimester system so 1 trimester (2.5months) of school, a 3-4 week break before the next one, all with different classes and lecturers. It is fast-paced so time flies! the turnover rate is high so don’t worry if there’s someone you don’t like. Was it academically hard? I’d say no, but many students still fail. if you listen in class and study with the resources provided you will be fine. There aren’t a lot of exams (if there were exams they are open book mostly) but a lot of reports and PRESENTATIONS. It’s ok if you’re not used to presentations now, by the time you graduate you will be a natural and you won’t be nervous anymore. You need to familiarise yourself with Australian academic writing (that’s how they do it here, not the American or British way so you need to be careful). Paragraphs MUST have a clear Topic, Explanation, Example, Evaluation/Analysis and Link!!!! Most of the assessments are marked both by Curtin Australia and Singapore, so it is generally fair. You 100% need a laptop, everything is online and nothing is on paper. Having a printer was useful but you don’t have to have one.

Students: Expect 98% of the students to be mainland Chinese. This place is the closest to a study abroad in China in Singapore. My Chinese skills improved exponentially being at Curtin and honestly I don’t hate it! I was immersed in Chinese language, culture, I learned a lot about different parts of China and Chinese food and snacks (my classmates were so generous!). There are other students from Myanmar, Indonesia, Malaysia, Vietnam, Russia, America but they are the minority (maybe maximum 5-6 non Mainland Chinese in each class). very little Singaporeans, maybe 2-3 in each class at most. If you don’t speak Mandarin Chinese the pool of potential friends is significantly narrowed but if you really want to you can make it work. Most students tend to keep to themselves unless you really try to talk to them, so If you are introverted and not very social it might not be easy for you to make friends. If you are an open person who loves people it won’t be a huge problem. Most people dress really well, but if you want to wear sweats that is okay too.

Location: A little far from central Singapore, but Singapore is quite small so it’s okay. There is a bus-stop right outside the building that goes straight to Orchard(45-60min), Holland Village and Vivocity(20min). So it is accessible! For international students, the area surrounding the school has a wide variety of condos but it is a bit pricey because of the location (close to National University Hospital and National University of Singapore). I stayed at Tanglin/Orchard which was less convenient but you can make it work.
The school is not big but it’s newly renovated, relatively luxurious and the washrooms are gorgeous. Not much food options (a cafeteria selling 10$ salads, sandwiches and pasta or a cafe selling bubble tea, oden, fried food and sandwiches). There are more food options further away. Classrooms are quite cold for Asian standards but normal for western standards.
If you have visa / bank / any problems the Student Connect is very helpful! Also the soundproof individual and group pods in the library are the best for studying.

Literally so many bad reviews on this school, the support system literally sucks, on the first few days of the trimester there's so many issues, so many staff & lecturers are unprepaid, so many attendance issues, academic issues that takes forever, to solve. Knowing that the school faces oveewhelming number of students with academic issues on the first week, there should be more staffs to assist instead of making student wait hours, on top of that academic issues need to be queued physically / physically take a queue number? why is there no online support / tech support? why is there no links to help and support and the timetable is absolute bullshit because why do they make students bid and choose if the school is just going to change the modules / schedule when the trimester / semester starts?! what's the point?? even after i graduate im pretty sure none of these issues will be solved or improve, wouldn't recommend anyone to come to this lousy school

compulsory 3 hour attendance for student pass holders every weekday. just go to other schools, dont apply here. people from other schools dont have to do this, just attend classes and attendance is counted from there. most students stay quite far and yet, is now forced to travel for this. this wastes our precious study time and transport money. avoid and go to other universities.

Very clean and well maintained. I went there for a seminar on 30th May about Machining Learning and AI at NUS School of Computing.

The event was held at COM 1 building and was well organised by both NUS and EMERITUS.

Besides SOC, I visited NUH and NUS School of Design and Engineering as well and was impressed by its cleanliness.

Can’t wait to possibly start a postgraduate program here 😶‍🌫️

Had a great experience today at NUS Open House 2023! Although everything was only taking place at UTown, it was an extremely vibrant and enjoyable experience. There were so many activities and booths around, and it was such fun and pleasure to be at the place!

Thoroughly enjoyed my 1-day outing to the campus, where students and staff alike are very welcoming, friendly, and awesome! Thank you NUS :)

P.S. it’s a great time to visit on weekends just to walk around and admire the architecture of the campus!

Your application website is trash. I don't know who wrote your website but the person did not pass computer science for sure. Your website logs out every 30 minutes and the problem is you don't save the progress that students have made. In the end they have to refill everything again and you know how long it takes to do that. You better solve that asap otherwise you will lose  amazing students.

Amazing place for students worldwide. Top notch facilities for everything you care about. really interesting lot of students to hang around. You'll love this space , the Campus is attracted all over the city of Singapore. For vegetarians, it's a bit tricky to get the desired food. Amazing public transport and AQI less than 50.

I was amazed when I visited this campus back in 2014. Still I'm wondering how an university campus can be so beautiful. This campus is the combination of the any modern shopping mall (e.g. vibocity) and a decent forest. One can enjoyed these both feeling once he/she inside of it. Everything is heavily organized and the campus is so bug that you can not visit the entire campus in a single day. The campus is full of stairs and that's why sometimes it is called national university of Stairs.

I got 10 /14 for my SW English weighted assessment 3. This was an improvement and my teacher in school praised me. My Geniebook teacher is very nice she just interacts with us. Her name is teacher Rose she explains the topics clearly and helps us with the areas we are not very good at.
Great experience. My Chinese tutor is very friendly and interactive. She also helped to improve on my speaking abilities.
The teachers are very fun and engaging. They are very passionate in teaching the students. The teachers always give extra classes to improve our learning. This helped me get full marks for math!

Afterskool provides me with so much worksheets and notes!! Every lesson is so fulfilling as I learn so much from each lesson :)) The facilities provided such as the study lounge is so conducive and this is the best tuition center!

I feel that afterskool is a great place for learning as the teachers are encouraging and motivating. Apart from that, the environment is perfect for studying whether you are in a class or revising on your own

We’re very thankful to the Science tutor Dr Gary for helping my daughter, Angle (Sec 2), improve so much in her Science. With his clear explanations and patient guidance, she gained more confidence and achieved great results. The tuition center are very clean and tidy good environment for having tuition ! Highly recommend him and KRTC !
The centre admin Ms Vonnee is very helpful and friendly. Teacher Mondira and Teacher Constance are very helpful and professional English teachers. My son enjoys his phonics class!

The place is clean
Dr Gary and Mr Eugene is very nice . I improve on my maths thanks to Mr Eugene .
The principal is also very friendly and helpful

The staff at this tuition centre assured me that they could find a tutor to teach my daughter on a specific day and timing even though their course schedule didn’t have this slot.  When the day for the first lesson came, there was no tutor there.  What a waste of our time!

We’re very happy with our son’s phonics class experience! The center manager is friendly and always welcoming, and Teacher Constance is experienced, patient, and knows how to engage young learners. I can see clear improvement in my son’s reading and confidence. The class size is small, which allows for more personal attention, and the overall environment is relaxed and child-friendly. Our son especially enjoys chatting with both teacher and center manager — he feels safe, happy, and looks forward to every class. Highly recommended!
Nice community library near benches at void deck

So glad to see it being filled up since the last reviews! Hope that this will grow into a hugggggeeeee community library someday

Just left a stack of books and hope that they will be read well by the community :)
Good solution interim before Tengah gets own library :)
Great idea 👍

Came across this lovely community library while doing a shoot and fell in love with the vibe. The vast collection of books across so many genres, the cosy atmosphere, and how neat and welcoming the space is — it’s truly special.

The residents have clearly put so much care into maintaining it. Even more amazed after learning about the history behind it from Kae Chee.

Please continue preserving this space. It’s more than just a library — it’s a home!

What an amazing experience at this beautiful community library setup by the local residences  ( wo any govt funding or subsidies ).    Today they organised a free lunch session to enable more socialising opportunities for the aging & needy communities living  @HollandV HDB residences.  Keep up the good work , it’s fantastic 🤩🤩🤩

A hidden treasure gem in the neighborhood of Holland Village. A small, quiet, and charming library, filled with a wide variety of books for all ages, with friendly neighbors keeping the community kampong spirit alive.

This is how HDB void decks could be put to good use: a public area, made meaningful and purposeful by the actual residents, for the community.

Definitely worth dropping by when you are in the neighborhood to see if you can find an old book from your childhood or check out the small collection of random manga in the corner.

Or just drop by and chill with the community cat.

A very homey and clean community library that caters to children, teens, adults and seniors! It was my first time visiting this library after seeing their IG posts. There are many books for all ages and genres to look out for, even book series. The residents and host are very friendly and showed me around the library. You can tell how much effort and care the residents put into keeping the space tidy and comfortable. The kampung spirit is very evident in this estate with regular ad-hoc events happening. Would visit again to see the array of books available!😆😆

I walked past Holland Village Little Library without realizing it

It is and still is a wonderful little corner under a HDB void deck

The residents have set it up themselves despite opposition from Officials SG bodies

It is a ground up initiative where residents gathered to chit chat instead of being cooped up in their urban cement apartments all days.

The place is well kept and breezy

There are library of books available.

There are also food rescue programs, plus gardening too

Gardening helps the human to connect with nature

A community space helps residents to connect with each other and cultivate social grace and a place of cohesion.

It is truly a wonderful place

The exterior looks cool and detailed
The tracks also need to be split just like other stations so that may cause problems on the NSL.
This station is scheduled to open in 2034. It is part of the NS line, between CCK and Bukit Gombak station.
Even though it haven't opened, the artist's impression looks wonderful.
What kind of a name is brickland bruh
Now I cannot park at BBDC because of this station Wa Lao

It’s March 2026 when I write this. There is upgrading work going on, it will probably go on till mid 2028.
The public toilets have been refurbished, new entrance gantries installed. There are two lifts so much easier access for wheelchairs and those carrying children

MRT station and bus interchange with connections! LRT connects NS line to DT line at Bukit Panjang. Coverred walk ways connecting with Lot One shopping mall.  Plenty of local delights and Western food shopping banking cinemas library portage community club supermarket and amenities nearby. Friendly fast attentive cosy comfy ambience for romantic intimates groups students tourists Expats and families.
The station is built but the design is yet to be created and still under construction

When approached a staff at SMRT help centre that was playing their phone, they were very rude. Asked simple question and was given attitude. What a joke. Next time dont put "WeCare" on the help centre.
A quiet interchange, but such an important one for Tengah residents. Grateful to all the bus captains and staff and vendors for keeping things running thank you❤️❤️❤️

Big, empty, airy. Some small eateries.

Main display that shows bus timing highly inaccurate. The main display timing vs individual display at the respective bus queue completely different. The buses seldom arrive on time, I.e 6 mins to next bus, then I see the bus now. Or it says bus arriving now, doesn’t arrive  and just bus just only come at the next slot. Highly unreliable.

At 14 march around 11.20 pm, I was at the service counter asking for advice on how to purchase a card and use it services. The chinese customer service officer was super rude, without even asking about my issue, he just dismissed me and ask me to go away. Super rude, I would give no rating to interchange if I have to, please don't be a service officer if you are this rude. Hope SMRT / SBS could change the person incharge of the office. In a newly developed town, this is the only interchange and with this kind of person in charge it is a disappointment.

Bus 872 timing is absurdly inconsistent particularly during peak hours. 872A also always comes with 872, making it a waste of the trip, then between 0730 to 0800hrs when there’s a lot more people but there’s no 872A to be seen but only single deck 872. Advertise 872A until so big but so ridiculously useless, really don’t understand how it can be such a failure.
Excellent facility with good number of toilet cubicles, canteen, juice vending and mama shop. Many berths for future expansion.
Very nice place for
Bus stop..

The driver drives at snail pace. Long long reaching end destinations.

Sunday 1pm, 31 May

Tengah Bus Interchange is finally open for business. The green color theme is very vivid to reflect Singapore's SG Bus's commitment to ensuring a smooth journey for all. Managed only by Tower Transit Singapore, there are currently only 3 trunk-service buses running from this newly opened bus Interchange - 870, 871, and 992 that connects Tengah with Bukit Batok, Bukit Gombak, Bukit Timah, and Jurong East.

As it is new, do expect least human activities as Tengah is still a live construction and the bus Interchange is meant to serve its first connectivity to other parts of Singapore.

Finally had a chance to visit this new town! There are many things I find unique such as the exit numbering similar to that of an MRT station and the NTWU canteen which looks a lot more like a coffee shop! This bus interchange would certainly be VERY beneficial to the people who are living or are moving nearby. Looking forward to coming here in the future and more people around. For now, it's quiet and peaceful.
10:1 tts poster to passenger ratio ahh structure clad in green

Expected to open on Sunday 21 July 2024, this interim bus interchange will be the first bus interchange in Tengah which is located at Tengah Boulevard near the junction with Tengah Park Avenue as well as Parc Woods @ Tengah residential estate to prevent confusion with the integrated transport hub which will be built at Tengah Town Centre adjacent to Tengah MRT Station on the Jurong Region MRT line. The interchange, which has three alighting berths and six boarding berths and is managed by Tower Transit Singapore under the Bulim Bus Package, will have three bus services: 870 (to Jurong Town Hall Bus Interchange), 871 (to Upper Bukit Timah Road near Beauty World MRT Station via Bukit Gombak) and 992 (to Bukit Batok Bus Interchange). The vehicular access to this bus interchange is at the signalised intersection with Tengah Boulevard and the bus interchange, which sits on a site which is reserved for residential use under the Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA).

Keat Hong LRT Station (BP3) is a light rail station along the Bukit Panjang LRT (BPLRT). Located within the Choa Chu Kang estate, the station is overground and located at the junction of Choa Chu Kang Way and Choa Chu Kang Avenue 1.

Taking its name from a former army camp in the vicinity (Keat Hong Camp), the station largely serves residential estates in the vicinity.

LRT station BP3 from Bukit Panjang MRT LRT conveniently located at Choa Chu Kang Way next to Keat Hong Mirage complex with popular Hey Sugar icecream Shop walking distance to Palm Gardens Condominium Keat Hong Quad Housing Complex Keat Hong Garden Park with children's playground jogging tracks sports facilities shade Pavilion strolling trails amphitheatres and breezy greenery away from busy cityscapes. Few LRT stops via South View LRT to Choa Chu Kang MRT LRT Lot One Mall with plenty of dinning shopping banking supermarkets library cinemas entertainments groceries sundries Electric Electronic telecommunication postage services household products community centre and amenities nearby. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students expats locals visitors and families.

Train delayed second time within 2 weeks time. Announcement totally not clear, no display in text on the delay and the reason. No announce on website on the delay either. Hard to imagine that this kind of level of service happen in developed country like singapore.
Clean and peaceful location. There are two platforms. When you go to the platform make sure you are in correct platform by checking the train direction sign boad to confirm your destination.

Opened on 6 November 1999 as part of the Bukit Panjang LRT line operated by SMRT Trains, this LRT station is located at Choa Chu Kang Way near the junction with Choa Chu Kang Avenue 1. The station brings direct rail connectivity for residents of Choa Chu Kang Neighbourhoods 1, 2 and 8 along Choa Chu Kang Central, Choa Chu Kang Avenue 1 and Teck Whye Lane along with the residents of Palm Gardens Condominium at Hong San Walk bringing them along to Teck Whye Garden, Teck Whye Shopping Centre as well as Choa Chu Kang Town Centre (Lot One, Choa Chu Kang Centre, Choa Chu Kang MRT/LRT Station and Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange). The bus services serving Keat Hong LRT station are SMRT Buses service 67 between Choa Chu Kang and Tampines, SMRT Buses service 188 and Express service 188e between Choa Chu Kang and HarbourFront, SBS Transit service 974 between Joo Koon and Bukit Panjang MRT/LRT Station, SMRT Buses service 976 between Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange and Bukit Panjang Bus Interchange and SMRT Buses Express service 982E between Choa Chu Kang Avenue 5 and Marina Boulevard. Keat Hong LRT station is named after the former army camp in the vicinity called Keat Hong Camp which was located at 261 Choa Chu Kang Avenue 1 before being relocated to 611 Old Choa Chu Kang Road as the land where the former army camp was located at was occupied by the Keat Hong Garden as well as the Keat Hong Axis, Keat Hong Quad and Keat Hong Mirage HDB flats.

place_name: Blk 253 Choa Chu Kang Market & Food Centre
review_text:
This would be my First and Last❗️

Went to this cai fan stall. And I’m shock when he told me the pricing for my plate is $5.20.

- sprouts with no meat or tofu (some stall I went do have with no additional charges)
- steam egg (no meat)
- char siew (he told me this cost $2.50 per serving !!!) ❗️as you can see from the photo, it very little meats given ❗️

⭕️ from the price list it mentioned $0.80 for vegetables, rice $0.60, meat $2.50 (the worker told me) which total should be $4.70. I don’t know how he calculated and it become $5.20.
- I don’t think mine is under selected veggie for both if yes also not accurate.

place_name: Blk 253 Choa Chu Kang Market & Food Centre
review_text:
Disappointed.
They charged $0.30 for a regular polystyrene takeaway box — not about the money, but is this even reasonable?
Skewers were tough and tasteless, and the crayfish didn’t taste fresh at all.
Overall a very underwhelming experience. Wouldn’t recommend or return.

place_name: Blk 253 Choa Chu Kang Market & Food Centre
review_text:
Yummy pancake:

Avoid the hum-jim-peng, the flour is lumpy and hardened when fried.

Totally unpalatable. I must give one star for the food.

Where is the ethic of food seller? This did not even cross the line of passable.

I advise they just focus on making  pancake & drop the fritters.

Approach with caution.

place_name: Blk 253 Choa Chu Kang Market & Food Centre
review_text: Went to the newly renovated Keat Hong Food Centre and Market on 2 Nov (Sat) and tried the zichar dishes from Hong Kong Street Old Chun Kee. Pretty solid dishes would be back again!

place_name: Blk 253 Choa Chu Kang Market & Food Centre
review_text: Dishonest seller at the kopitiam traditional Bak Chor Mee stall. Owner was alone working as a cashier and cooking the food and intentionally keyed in my noodle type so he could charge me twice for takeaway container (2x30 cents). They also covered the menu dish showing the on-going promotion so that people would order from their regular menu. Noodles don’t taste good and expensive, very soggy. There are many better options around.

Drinks are cheaper here compared to other food courts.
Fish in this set from halal stall was freshly fried which was impressive but I could only taste the 'salt in it. Chillies was good, egg was done to my liking. Ikan bilies were normal.

I ordered char siew roast meat curry noodles from wang wang roast meat store, but received curry chicken noodles instead. The meat was completely drenched in curry, so it wasn’t obvious until I poured it out at home. Disappointing to be charged more but given a cheaper alternative.

The staff at this store selling Ah Ma Curry Chicken were very rude and inconsiderate. I purchased Ah Ma curry chicken with rice and bread before I collected the bowl was 3rd quarter full only portion is very small total I paid $8 and politely requested extra curry gravy. However, the lady refused in a very rude manner and instigating the male cook not to give extra curry gravy. As a result, I returned half of the bread which I don’t have enough gravy to eat along. Still can ask me to go ahead to complain them 😓

Such service staff should not be allowed to operate without proper training. They need to be trained in basic customer service and shown how to be more considerate toward customers. Some even did not wear masks. NTUC management should take action somehow this outlet is lack of service and inconsiderate mostly operate by foreigners.
5 stars for the steamed fish stall. This is my favourite stall and the only one I keep buying from. Huge portion of fish. Super value for money.

I recently visited the newly renovated food center owned by the well-known chain brand, Kopitiam, and I must say, it was a delightful experience!

The food center is clean and airy, featuring spacious seating and large ceiling fans that create a comfortable dining environment. It's a perfect spot to enjoy a meal without feeling cramped.

Food choices are impressive, with a variety of stalls offering delicious options. If you have the Fairprice app, you can enjoy discounts when ordering at the food stalls, which is a nice bonus!

The kopi (coffee) was light, so if you prefer a stronger brew, be sure to order the kopi gao for that thick, rich flavor.

I particularly enjoyed the famous Jian Bo Shui Kueh and Chee Cheong Fun, absolutely loved them! My kids opted for the tosai which was equally good.

Highly recommended for families looking for an affordable and comfortable place to have breakfast. With its great food and inviting atmosphere, it’s definitely worth a visit!
The indian food is really good at affordable price and friendly staffs!
The Indian food stall is quite good. Hot drinks from the main drink stall needs improvement.
Home made kind of food. Good option for vegetarians.
The whole place is halal, even the chinese food. Can easily find a place to sit even though it is peak lunch time.
All food are halal. Area is air conditioned, the most popular stall would be the indian stall. The gram masala tea is really good

Been a Hot 🥵 Morning &
Glad to indulge on a refreshing matcha cloud. Milk 🥛 on point and feels like Oat. Matcha 🍵 tastes great too. Happy to have a Neighbor close by that serves matcha to
The community 👌
The matcha cloud was really creamy, along with the soft, moist matcha cookies. Another occasion, I ordered the plain matcha drink. It was slightly sweet n refreshing. Cute packaging too
Ordered a Strawberry Basque Cheesecake for Mother's Day and it did not disappoint. The whole family love it from young to old. Will definitely support again for other festive specials!

Great matcha latte, although it could use a bit if sweetness to bring out a richer flavour.
Owner was very friendly and decorated the exterior beautifully!
Drink is quite refreshing and it taste excellent.

I followed the scent of roasted beans,
past gates wrapped in quiet green.
The morning glowed, fresh, inviting,
a day that promised something sweet.

But the guard smiled with patient eyes,
“Sorry! parents only. Closed during school holidays.”

Her words fell softly, yet the sigh
within me brewed like cooling tea.
The chairs were waiting, just beyond,
the chatter locked in holiday sleep.

I lingered, hopeful, by the pond,
dreaming of the sip I couldn’t keep.

Love the broccoli basket, can’t live without it; my only source of vitamin A, C, and K. The owner singlehandedly performed a tracheotomy using the straw from my iced mocha. Would sell my left kidney for the broccoli baskets, and give up my first born child to the owner. Thankful and blessed for the Heritage Cafè. Live. Laugh. Love.

Oh, Heritage Café, haven of delight,
Your doors a portal to a world of grace,
Where scents of spice and sweets the soul ignite,
And flavors linger, time cannot erase.

The owner, radiant, a timeless muse,
Her charm, a melody that hearts ensnare,
With every smile, the sun's warmth she imbues,
And every word, a symphony laid bare.

Her hands, like artists, craft the finest fare,
Each dish a masterpiece, a tale retold,
A blend of love and care beyond compare,
An heirloom’s taste more precious than pure gold.

Oh, Heritage Café, your magic's true,
With her, the queen, who makes the old anew.

I threw up after drinking their tap water infused ice lemon tea. it was traumatising. I had to walk up to chinese class after throwing up. It was disturbing.

However, they have some good hot chocolate. So it's been compensated.
Luv the coffee so much, Ik they wanna us to keeping full of energy so they just put the unlimited sugar,full of luv

hands down , the most delicious and most addictive spuds ever tasted . fell in love with their spuds from the very first day i ordered till today !

p.s. BRING BACK LOADED MASH BOWL LAH PLEASEEE 🥺🥺🥺

5 Stars!!! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ First met Abang Cino at Ayam Gepok Anakmak in AMK and he recommended me to try his loaded spuds. Decided to give it a try and it did not disappoint!

Generous toppings, great flavours and a perfectly cooked russet potato that was filling and satisfying! You can tell effort goes into every order!

Highly recommended if you're looking for a good loaded spud! Will definitely order again! 👍🏻🍠🔥
Ordered Minced Chicken and taste was superb along with bolognese sauce. I regret that I only ordered one! Hahaha 👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼Looking forward to try other menu.

1st time ordered Spud from JackPodSG today 25/04/2026. I order Mince Beef, Classic and crabmeat. Eventually, all the 3 spud taste delicious. The ingredients also many and at a affordable price. For those guys out there, what are you waiting for? Come try and support JackPodSG. Highly recommended 5 stars! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Is very yummy and  delicious .
I like the beef so much .
The food make me very full 💯

This is a review after 3 visits trying different types of food. Service is casual and friendly, and ambience is wonderfully refreshing set in the cooler and  fresher air of Tengah farms area.

The grilled food is consistently good  with the grill imparting a nice smokey fragrance and flavor to the grilled chicken 5⭐ and sausages (wonderfully juicy and we'll seasoned , the chicken satay 5⭐(highly recommended + good peanut sauce), grilled prawns 4⭐(a little dry but amazing chilli 🌶️🔥sambal dip 5⭐).

The freshness of the accompanying salad vegetables and the fruit rojak 4.5⭐ is evident (maybe farm fresh?). The mushrooms 5⭐ accompanying the grilled meats were really good.

Other notable mentions:
Fish and chips 4.5⭐
Soft shell crab pasta 4⭐
Chicken wings 5⭐

A hidden gem that is a little tricky to get to without own transport but well worth the visit.

If you think Singapore doesn't have a countryside, think again. This might be one of the only few places to get away from the city. This cafe is quite cozy and offers fairly good food. I recommend to give the chilli crab pasta a try. While you wait for your food, go take a quick look at the plant nursery next door; great variety at affordable prices.

Really enjoyed the lamb! It's meat falls off the bone, and is almost crispy while juicy. The creamy mashed potato with fried seaweed was delicious, and overall has a striking tomato flavour and acidity.

The classic satay is cooked to perfection, and gives me vibes of being in batam.

The clam and prawn pasta was cooked well, but the sauce slips off the pasta hahhaa. The gyudon meat was thin and fatty, the rice was a bit overseasoned and dry.

Service is good, the server brought out a propeller to fan a persistent fly away from our table xD! And helped us navigate the online ordering system.

Open environment, but hard to get here without private transport. Has parking, and the washrooms are maintained well. Good place to bring someone to, has a live band after 7pm.

3 mains, 1 side and 3 drinks set us back $122, but thr was no regrets on our end.

Had our Company Christmas Gathering in Ten+, away from busy urban area - not buildings etc. Peaceful place when you arrive.  Thankful to YL, Fabian and all yr staffs.  Great hospitality.  The arrangement was centralised for us to mingle around. Also thanks to the live-band, actively have fun w us. We have positive feedback in Good Food n Good service.  Laksa live stations n BBQ food! We have enjoyed our evening.  Thanks again.  Merry Christmas n Happy New Year!

Satay, Prawn Pasta, Fries, & Seabass - all the food was goos but the sambal sea bass was the highlight for me. Skin was crispy and nice, sambal in point, sea bass tasted juicy and fresh.

Atmosphere was so good, so relaxing. The live band didnt overpower the venue so it was still possible to have conversations with friends. The live band on Friday was amazing.

This is truly a hidden gem. Would definitely be back again.

Tried Kopi jjan for the first time today, the owner Noel was super friendly and smiley. Had the mocha bear, pinklet and the wafu stuck, everything was so good and did not disappoint. The mocha had a generous amount of chocolate bits too. If you’re living in Tengah then you should definitely give them a try, will not regret!
Wide variety of unique drinks catered to all age types! Crispy freshly baked waffles with huge serving of nutella!

The drinks are one of a kind and can’t be found anywhere else in the world. The Matcha (Torteas) drink is my favourite with a rich matcha taste that is easy to drink. The watermelon (Pinklet) looks really pretty and definitely should try at least once if you are a Bandung lover. Will come back to try the other drinks that they have

Really liked this drink from KoPi Jjan ☕️ Smooth, rich, and not too sweet — very easy to enjoy. It felt comforting and you can tell it’s well made.

The cup design was cute too, which was a nice touch. Definitely something I’d order again. Will totally get it again!
Very delicious waffles with creamy sauces. Tried most of KoPi Jjan drinks. All that we tasted are nice and refreshing ❤️❤️ Definitely will order often!!
1 March 2026: A newly opened large complex featuring a polyclinic, food court with numerous options, Ntuc Fairprice, Anytime Fitness and many other shops. High ceiling and good ventilation.

The Luckin Coffee in Parc Point just opened today, 6th May. It has outdoor and indoor seating and is really cosy. Seating is also well spaced out. What's really attractive now is the $0.99 first drink offer and for the size of the coffee, really cheap. For those who already get their caffeine dose from Luckin, they should be familiar as the usual flavours are all there. Guess its going to be a favourite hang out for the crowd here. I think the only downside is the need for an app, saw a few customer (elderly plus workers) coming in and enquiring but could not buy from the counter straight, thus left in disappointment as the $0.99 probably was a really good offer.
big store. indoor and outdoor seating. only 1 kiosk. no counter. no much people cause just open. fast service. got grab and panda delivery.

Visited on second day of official opening of Parc Point, only food court and Fairprice supermarket are opened. Looking for rest of shops to open. Food in the food court Gourmet Paradise by Koufu Group, is so so nothing amazing.

Only the FairPrice, Anytime Fitness and Food court is open for now. The place is an open-air concept mall, so plenty of natural ventilation and plenty of seating arrangement to laze around. The mall comes with a rooftop garden. It also has a National University Polyclinic at the foodcourt level 2.

Excited and looking forwards to its full opening.

A bustling neighborhood mall! This mall is very convenient since it's directly linked to the Clementi MRT and bus interchange. It’s got everything you need across several floors, from a variety of shops and banks to a supermarket, food court, and even a library. It’s the perfect all-in-one spot for locals to grab a meal or run errands.

The Clementi Mall is a very convenient small-to-medium sized heartland mall, especially because it is directly connected to Clementi MRT station and the bus interchange. This makes it one of the easiest malls to access in the west, and it naturally gets very crowded on weekends and during peak hours

The tenant mix is practical and useful, with food options, shops, services, a supermarket, pharmacies, and casual dining places. It is not a huge destination mall, but it works very well for daily needs, quick meals, errands, and meeting up with family or friends

The lower floors can feel quite packed, especially near the MRT entrance and popular food areas. The upper floors are noticeably less crowded, so it is more comfortable if you want to walk around or browse at a slower pace

The Clementi Mall: A very convenient and modern shopping hub.

I visited around 7 PM on a weekday. The mall is directly connected to the MRT station, making it extremely convenient for a quick stop or a dinner visit.

The Shops & Atmosphere:
There is a great variety of shops and restaurants, including a section with many food stalls that feels like a mini-market. I was very surprised to find a massive library right inside the mall! It’s a great place to browse and borrow books in a modern setting.

The Food & Culture:
One "minus point" for me is that the mall mostly contains international brands and chain stores. I didn't find many small, local shops, so it might not be the best place if you are looking to explore traditional Singaporean culture.

The Verdict:
It is a perfect, air-conditioned spot for practical shopping and dining, but it feels more like a standard modern mall than a cultural destination.

This mall is small but very high-traffic because of the students and the MRT connection. It’s functional and has all the basics like a big library and plenty of quick-service food. If you want a more authentic experience, the Clementi 448 Market nearby is where the "real" West side food is, though it’s always hot and noisy.

Clementi mall is a place my family would go almost every weekends.
A place where we enjoy most of the local food at B1.  The recent visit at the B1 food area is disappointed, the indoor tempetature made us stay away of the poor aircon ventilation.

A convenient neighbourhood supermarket that carries most daily essentials. You can easily find bread, eggs, vegetables, fruits, baby items (milk & diapers) and basic household items. The store is generally well stocked and organised, making it easy to shop quickly. Prices are reasonable as well.

Previously bought some fruits here and found that a few pieces inside were already rotten when I got home. However, the staff handled it well and allowed a 1-to-1 exchange when I brought it back. Appreciate the service recovery.

I visited this Sheng Siong outlet in the district of Tengah today. I was impressed by its wide variety of products and how well stocked it was. The shopping aisles here were quite wide and the air-conditioning here was more than adequate - all of which adds to a very nice shopping experience.

(Edit: Wrote a feedback to the store and they actually called back within 2 hours. In this day and age, that's pretty good service. Hope they'll start carrying more stuff that they put out in their promo posters.)

Some products under promo or with discounts found on your website are not available in this branch. All drinking water but no distilled water. Ha to go to Giant to find distilled water. Asked the staff and they had no idea what the product manager is doing.
Photo taken at Giant, in case the poor product manager has no idea the difference.

Your staff can learn to be more polite to customers and not to show their sickening rude attitude.I am sure the management of sheng siong  has a reputation to maintain right?!So this is not acceptable to me at all!We are paying customers alright,we are not getting things for free and your staff are working for a salary right?So learn to give respect and take respect!
Thank you so much for helping me. Also begin so patient with me. Joy Tang 👍👍

A newly opened FairPrice in Tengah that feels modern and well organised. The store has a clean, futuristic layout with clearly arranged sections, making it easy to find groceries and everyday essentials.
Shelves are neatly stocked, and the overall shopping environment feels spacious and comfortable. The staff are also friendly and helpful if you need assistance.
A convenient and pleasant place for nearby residents to do their regular grocery shopping.

Finally, an NTUC FairPrice in Tengah 🙌

It’s not a huge outlet, but it covers all the essentials such as bread, eggs, meat, fruits, vegetables. There’s even a live prawn section, which is quite impressive for a smaller store. Although… be prepared for some excitement because a few prawns were literally jumping out of the tank 😂 Fresh indeed.

Since it’s a relatively compact NTUC, don’t expect an ultra wide variety or specialty items. The selection is more practical than fancy.

One downside though is the queue can get pretty long and moves rather slowly, especially during peak hours.

Overall, still a solid addition to Tengah. Convenient, reliable, and finally gives residents another option besides the usual two.

Oh, and this ntuc outlet is NOT 24/7.

Poor layout leads to small and poorly utilized space.

No pick your own chicken (at Giant)
No feminine shaver products,
Limited wine selection
Cannot find beef (was it even there??)
Snacks were ALREADY Lou hong on the first day
Fruits much better quality at plantation plaza Giant

Small and not efficiently utilized, Giant is better.
It’s alright, a little small, can be larger hehe… Anything is better than Giant imo. Please expand.
Staff are very good and helpful. Place not to big or small. Just nice. Layouts are well planned and easy to find. Great job tengah fairprice.

Somehow this little Indian grocer in Clementi West has been my go to place for all Indian type of veggies and fruits.
The coriander is always available, and a good quantity of my favourite karpooravali bananas are brought in every Friday or Saturday.
It's mango season now and Alphonso mangoes are here too!
In the fridge they even have Australian Greek yoghurt which is such a pleasant find.
The guys may not speak a lot of English but they are polite, helpful and know their products well.

Best neighbourhood grocery shop in west coast - especially for Indians! You might have to recheck the expiry dates before buying any products coz at times they might not have cleared out the old stocks!

The only Indian store near Clementi which has an assortment of products from Indian groceries to Body grooming. If you are an Indian and living near the area, there’s no reason not to go here. Better than going all the way to Little India for the same stuff.

The orders for delivery is old stock and lentils came with insects in them. Most of the chappathis were gone and had a yellow stain on all of them. We did not ask them to take it back or refund... When asked for an exchange for the wrong lentils and the lentils that had insects, the lady gets aggressive and is extremely short tempered. I've sent pictures if the items in less than 5 mins (of the insects and wrong items) of the delivery. They refused to come back and take it.
Easy pick up store

Since moving back to the west from the east, Sheng Siong has become one of my usual supermarkets that I frequently get my groceries from. The supermarket is spacious with a wide variety of selections, and everything is always neatly arranged. The vegetables and fruits are fresh as well.

The staff there are also very friendly, especially two ladies whom I encountered.  Darchana and Santos are very helpful and professional. Darchana always smiley and very helpful whenever assistance is needed. Small gestures like these really make the shopping experience more comfortable and pleasant.

I was in a rush so I didn’t manage to take more pictures, but I’ll update with more photos the next round.

Cashiers are fast and friendly. Will remind customers if they are Chas or Pioneer on the designated days.

The 2 storey Sheng Siong is 24 hours. Appreciate the long operating hours.

Peaceful shopping can be done after mid night.

Early in the morning, there's staff to help butcher pork to ur desire. Choose a piece of meat and they minced for u. Pork Belly if not much people, they can slice it for u.

Fish counters operate till later timing then pork. Large selection of prawns, fish, crabs.

Outdoor area, there's plant section, veg section and promotion section.

Variety is huge!

Efficient, tidy, with enormous range of food, groceries, household appliances and sanitary products that you might not find in normal grocery stores.

The clean and well lit enterprise also keeps jaywalkers and suspicious people away, there used to be provocatively dressed women harassing motorcyclists for cigarettes years ago. They and an ever rising count of shoplifters have been dealt with swiftly by the authorities.

It helps that the staff are very friendly and welcoming to all cultures and preserve the Singapore spirit of orderly and honest business.

24-hour supermarket at West with a wide range of food & many varieties. Never worry about being unable to buy fresh seafood cod this outlet 24 hours yet can find almost everything we need. From food to household items, too

I love supermarkets, especially the bigger ones. I miss the old Sheng Siong Supermarket at Tekka Mall, which had unfortunately closed years ago.
This outlet seems rather big to me; Spacious with a great selection of affordable daily essentials. A really good place for grocery shopping.

** 4% special discount for seniors (Singaporeans/ PRs) 60 and above, every Tuesday and Wednesday.
**The Tem Tem Protein Tempeh Chips sold here are suitable for vegetarians, healthy and super delicious. Get yours now for the coming Chinese New Year. You’re worth pampering.

Mart stocks fresh bananas and vegetables, a wide variety of condiments/spices and pantry items, household items (detergent, deodorant, shampoo, etc.), lots of snacks, ice cream, and of course your usual drinks. It also stocks different items from the NTUC express across the road, and several items are cheaper. They carry more Indian-loved items, but you won’t know if you like it till you try it. Doesn’t hurt that the husband-wife team is humble and friendly. Support small businesses people.

Best Asian grocery in Town !!!! Good thing about this is having updates every time stock replenishes.

Management is way too helpful. Very much recommended.

EZ minimart is a great convenience store. They have a wide variety of items, including snacks, drinks, groceries, and even some prepared foods. The prices are reasonable and the staff is friendly and helpful. Most Indian groceries are available
Great service, fresh groceries, very kind shop owner.

Excellent service and good collection of all items. Vegetables, Indian Groceries, Daily essentials, Snacks and Drinks etc. - Grab anything easily at this wonderful EZ Mini Mart. Right one at the right place in right hands. Wish you more and more success. ❤️

Cashier was cold and indifferent, but isn't that rude in a sense. Just stoic.

It is a good pit stop for you to purchase any essentials, such as umbrellas, sustenance et cetera. The stall is small yet packed with essentials, so it is a good pit stop regardless. You can stop by here anytime to purchase any items that you may need along your travel.
Staff may have changed, she was polite and helped address my questions. Had no problems getting my food here. The store is a bit small so not everything you usually expect from 7-11 would be here.
Very rude the lady cashier,she don't like people but the items if ask anything also she not happy and don't like to explain

The night time staff is horrible. Her attitude is like she hates her job. And tired of customers that walk in to purchase things. Shes only nice when her drunk friends are relaxing next door from the store. Unfortunately i have to put a star 🌟 just to put this review.

Very rude and unhelpful staff, Linda. She was in the store when I entered. While making my purchase, I asked her where is the nearest toilet and she shouted at me, coffee shop, coffee shop! I don’t owe you a living, woman! Learn how to speak properly if you cannot speak professionally. Or if you don’t like your job, look for something else you enjoy.

4 stars because have Sheng Siong. Other than the 2 mini-mart like market stall (one run by Chinese aunty and another by an Indian auntie), others sell vegetables or typical produce. Also available are the stalls selling Chinese prayer items. Used to have a halal meat produce stall that sells marinated satay meat, but since renovation can't seem to find the stall anymore. Some previous vendor like the stall selling fresh noodles and tofu have shifted to the hawker/market at Bangkit.

Fajar wet market is relatively small with 2 fresh pork stalls, 2 vegetable stall, a fresh YTF stall, 1 Indian and 1 Chinese provision shop. Here's also the Fajar SC with Sheng Siong and Giant, both 24hr; Macdonald and 7eleven. more shops... selling cake n bread, mobile phone, medical hall, ABC shop, a coffee shop and a AC food court. SG Poolz, music school at the 2nd level
Got 2 supermarket n wet market. Comprehensive choice of groceries n 2 coffeeshops for hawker food.
A neighborhood convenient center that has food centre, supermarket, wet market, hard ware shop and many more
Went for service koufu's automatic door

Went to the outlet (Tagore) to place an order for CNY, to make sure that delivery would be in place for reunion dinner.

Food was delivered just before the scheduled delivery time, which was exactly what we needed. Quality was good and tasted fresh. The fishballs and beef balls were especially well received.

Will use them again next time we do a steamboat.

What a great wet market, combined with Sheng Siong, lots of independent small stores, food court. The prices are fantastic compare to NTUC Fair price at the wet market. There’s heap of hawker food too, especially busy were prata, chicken rice, Lor mee and bee hoon and curry chicken. Nice atmosphere in the weekend and old nostalgia where your ah-Ma would love to shop here. Just wished it had more meat and vegetable stores in the wet market section. Good place to show the kids and spend the morning in the weekend.

I highly recommend this butcher!  Prime Pork ribs are very high quality, smooth, tender and fresh.  Their minced pork is the perfect meat/ fat ratio, very fresh.  No rancid smell at all.  I’ve also purchased their deboned chicken (quarter) 500gm pack many times- used it for grilling, steaming, braising.  Also recommend their pork meat balls- very tasty, has cuttlefish in it.  They deliver the items in an insulated box, full of ice packs to keep the meat cold and safe.  Everything is vacuum packed.  I’m so pleased with their service.  Their pricing is also competitive.  The thing with going to the wet market physically, you still need to repack into your desired portions upon coming home, so ordering from quan shui really saves me a lot of time and energy.

I always love going to the wet markets but cannot afford the time to wake up super early to get the freshest produce! Love that they deliver to your doorstep and even give the option to recycle the ice packs/boxes. The selection of produce is great, quality is really good and worth every bit of the slight premium!! Fruits & meat are super fresh! Ready to eat section is great for quick meals too! 100% will recommend!

We cooked with the wild caught balai threadfin for porridge, Silverfern short rib shabu shabu for Korean beef Bulgogi, silverfern ribeye steak for Beef bowl. It was amazing! 👌

We’ve been buying from Quan Shui Wet Market for a few times, and each time a satisfying one. Their items are fresh and clean, reached us in good condition and packaging. Complimentary delivery is reliable and good! Joyce from customer service is friendly and helpful. So far we’ve purchased almost all types of what they are selling and we’ll still purchase from them for long. Thank you Quan Shui Market!

Amazing staff here, they helped with all my queries and application to the gym!

Equipments are new and great. Would highly recommend this gym for your next workout!

The newly opened gym at Parc Point in Tengah. The facility looks modern and well equipped, with a good range of cardio machines, strength equipment, and free weights.
The space is clean and well organised, making it comfortable for both beginners and regular gym-goers.
Like other Anytime Fitness locations, members can access the gym 24/7, which is convenient for different schedules.
A good addition to the neighbourhood for residents looking for a convenient place to work out. 💪
4 squat racks, nice vibe, helpful, friendly and attentive staff!
first time here with a friend and we loved it! super clean, great vibes, and a solid range of machines. the staff were really friendly too! appreciate the warm welcome! 😊

Had a very positive experience with Bella at Anytime Fitness. She was friendly, welcoming, and professional throughout the entire session. Bella gave us a detailed tour of the gym, clearly explained the different membership plans, and shared the benefits of joining AF.

A spacious and well-equipped fitness corner with plenty of stations for strength and obstacle-style training. Great for calisthenics, bodyweight workouts, and maybe even practising for Ninja Warrior haha. Nicely surrounded by greenery too.

Went there for table tennis. Table 5 is ok to play but table 4 spacing is smaller. Very hot, too. A few badminton courts behind the tables. Also, went there today (2-Aug-25) for Pesta Sukan 2025 Table Tennis. During this, the whole hall was used for the event with 14 tables and air-conditioned; hence, quite ok to play.
Newly renovated. Everything looks good.

Thankgod for this water park in the Summer, it’s old af but still works.
Wave pool - occurs every 30 minutes only. Circular pool is constant and relaxing, pool entry is purchased from a machine with the assistance of an employee. No idea why they are there as we can all purchase a ticket from a machine.
Entry staff looked lazy and were stuck on their phones whilst pool lifesavers were 100% focused on swimmers, thank you.

Amazing place
U can spend your whole day in just 1 or 2 dollars just with tap your mrt card
