It was my first time there. Although it’s a little far for me but it is worth joining the activity. There are many other facilities & activities and prices are affordable. My nephew enjoyed the 3 days football camp.

I was here for a community event this Sunday morning. And it was also my very first time in Tengah. Getting there was easy, albeit with lots of construction nearby. Public parking seems to be free - at the moment. I had lunch at the Burger King outlet at Tengah CC - it was not bad. However, when I used the public toilets at Tengah CC, it was in disrepair. Quite sad, as I believe it is still a new CC.

As a resident of Tengah Plantation, I passed by the new Tengah CC a few times on my way to bus stops. Decided to check out what's brewing and was welcomed by a friendly and cheerful counter staff on duty yesterday morning. I believe her name is Stella. By now there are already many varieties of courses. Definitely looking forward to joining some classes once I can get my schedule to coincide. Keep the courses coming and love the vibe!

Basketball court is seldom open due to the amount of events going on. Court is locked for few days before the event for "setup" but there is no set up that can be seen going on at all. Dont understand the reason for this. Appreciate the events being planned but is there a better place to hold the event rather than the facilities that are in high demand?
The greenery setup around Tengah Community Centre is astonishing. A bit warm on Sunny days but perfect in the evening to drop by the neighborhood for a meal

CC with 2 food options Burger King and 1 Prata Joint.
Nice indoor basketball court with roof top garden, also comes with a GYM (Anytime Fitness), easily accessible!

New Revamped Jurong East Swimming Complex with Lazy and waterplay areas for the kids. Hv a good wave generation pool which operates every hour.A bit sad that the water slides has been replaced with a new children water play area for toddlers and young children. Not enough shaded canopies for users of the facility to keep their belongings the heat on hot sunny days. No food vending machines for snacks, only hv a drinks vending machine.
Fun time with kiddos. The toilets are great with large cubicles and compartments to hang your dry clothes. Definitely a comeback for my family.

Although I must say that I missed the huge slides that my generation grew up with. But it is a nice transitional change to me being a Dad now, bringing my little kiddos in what is now a kids play area.

Also the toilets have had a face-lift. Kids changing room is nice and clean. Adults changing room is equally good.

They had a baby pool, kids playground, kids splash area, lazy river and a wave pool. Wave pool had waves every hour lasting about 15mins each time. Lazy river is where we spent most of our time at, easy going and fun. Rates are affordable with activesg. Minus one star because I'd prefer having the slides.

Went with family on a Saturday. Place was busy but still have space for a good swim. Kids enjoyed the new water play area and wave pool (though wave was mild and very far apart). The lazy river is still there.

Bukit Batok Swimming Complex is located within the HDB neighbourhood.

I went to swim there during the weekend. Do note that there are some lanes being used for training.

The swimming pool's facilities are well maintained and clean so far. There's a decent pool area for the kids.

The shower head is very powerful. Similar to other public swimming pools, there's no heater. There's toilet specially for kids. For the female toilet, there's this adjustable hair dryer, which is pretty cool. I don't need to hold it while it dries my hair. You need to put your hand over the sensor for it to sense, and it will start blowing. However, it might stop after a while, so you need to wave your hand again for it to work.

There are lockers if you want to lock your belongings. If not, you can put it along the seats of the pool.

There's also water cooler with icy water for you to refill your bottle or drink directly.

Overall, it's a good experience swimming in Bukit Batok Swimming Complex.

Complex has been around for many decades, almost iconic. There is a fun pool for children too! HDB parkkng available nearby.
Unfortunately, there is only 1 entrance to the complex. So always have to walk one big round if parked at nearby HDB.

- S$1.3 for adult on weekdays.
- The changing room is clean and spacious.
- It is a little far from changing room to competition pool, a little crowded on Monday evening
- There are 3 types of pool: competition, training and kid.
Great swimming class for kids. Coach is patient, friendly, and professional. My kids gained confidence and improved quickly. He also looks forward to the water playground after class. Recommended.

I would not recommend to go there when you go there first time and let them know you are visitor. They treat you differently when we enter before paying everything we told them we are visitor coming first time and nobody informed us about swimming suits, but after we paid suddenly, they let us know that you need swimming suit. This is like a totally scam and taking out the money they could have told us before entering instead of telling us after paying I would not recommend at all the staff was not cooperating at all

First time at EagleWings Cinematics, after having a great dinner at EagleWings Loft a few months back.

First impressions were excellent. The staff warmly showed us around the lounge and explained how we could enjoy the place before the movie. We were especially excited to order the lobster risotto again, which we had been craving.

The lounge experience was affected when a rowdy group arrived before what seemed to be a company movie screening. Not the cinema’s fault, but it would help if staff could gently guide such groups to available seats instead of allowing them to block walkways and inconvenience other guests.

Our sides arrived quickly, though the risotto took a little longer. Staff were attentive throughout and checked with us on whether we preferred to dine in the lounge or in the hall.

The Platinum Hall was beautifully furnished and comfortable. Our food was neatly arranged at our seats when we entered. One thing to note is that the top-row “sofa” seats do not have centre armrests, unlike the lower rows, which may matter depending on seating preference.

Before the movie started, a family of three occupied two sofa sections, and the child was climbing around and making quite a bit of noise. Thankfully, it stopped once the movie began after the finally parents stepped in. It did make me wonder whether there are checks to ensure guests are seated according to their bookings.

Overall, it was still a very enjoyable evening, and I would gladly return again, hopefully without another large corporate screening crowd while in the lounge, or rowdy children in the theatre.

Made a trip today to try out EagleWings Cinematics for the first time, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. While we initially encountered some hiccups with the lounge service, the manager and team were proactive, sincere, and prompt in addressing our concerns, which we truly appreciated. The theatre itself was well-maintained, with spacious and comfortable seating. Dining in the theatre was enjoyable, and the Loft Menu offered good food and snacks at reasonable prices. Given the many positive aspects of our visit, we look forward to returning.

Everything was good but the service of the food counter staff on duty today. We ordered popcorn from the QR code in our seats, but after 15-20 mins it still had not arrived. We went out and saw that your staff Madison who was at the popcorn counter was playing with her phone and did not take notice of our order. Re-training highly encouraged.

Amazing experience. We booked in advance the premium lounge. My son and I used their massage chairs. That alone was worth the experience. It was like getting a deep tissue massage. They seem to have upgraded their massage chairs recently. Oh and free flowing drinks (hot or hold). (I recommended u come at least 45mins before the cinema start time). Then we went in, each two people can sit together on a couch which reclines and allows you to stretch your legs. It even comes with a fresh blanket in a pouch. Then u can scan the QR code and order as if you’re at a restaurant with a garment menu. Optionally you can also order popcorn 🍿and pay as u order. or press a separate call button to order more free soft drinks.

Royal experience. We watched supermario galaxy movie and the sound system and the screen size was suitable for the size of the room.

I realized that we picked the best cinema in SG when we kept talking about how awesome the experience for 10mins while we walked back to the MRT. We started planning our next visit as soon as we walked out.

Oh and the kindness and the hospitality is something else. This great level of treatment of customers is a rare treat in Singapore.

I wish I can give it higher than 5 stars
⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ it’s obvious whoever came up with this idea loves cinema, food and wants to share their passion with other people in SG. I wish this place more success and expansion in SG.

Kitty and I have been loyal patrons of EagleWings Cinematics for years, and we absolutely adore this cinema.  It's a truly unique experience that we keep coming back to again and again.

EagleWings Cinematics is located within the KAP Mall, and it stands out from other cinemas in Singapore because of its intimate and luxurious atmosphere.  Each theater is cozy and designed to hold a small group of people, like 12 to 20 people, making it feel like a private screening. It's so comfortable and relaxing!  The seating is fantastic, and we've tried all their classes—Silver, Gold, and Platinum.  Our favorites are Gold and Platinum, which offer plush reclining sofas with blankets to keep you warm.

But the real highlight is their amazing service and amenities.  Right next to the cinema is a fantastic restaurant where you can order a wide variety of food, from local favorites like laksa to international dishes like steak and fish and chips.  You can even order beer if you choose Gold or Platinum class.  It's a far cry from the typical cinema snacks! You can also enjoy their lounge one hour before your movie starts, with free-flowing coffee and soft drinks, and even two massage chairs to relax in!

The service is impeccable.  They efficiently deliver your food to your table in the theater, and the table lamps are perfectly positioned to illuminate your meal without disturbing other guests.  The sound system is excellent, and the air conditioning is just right.  It's a comfortable and enjoyable movie-going experience.

We love how EagleWings never feels crowded or rowdy.  The atmosphere is always so peaceful and relaxing, perfect for enjoying a movie.  We've recommended EagleWings to many of our friends, and they've all become addicted to this amazing cinema.  From that point on, they've been watching movies at EagleWings exclusively!

The overall experience is always positive.  It gives you a holistic experience, where you can enjoy your movie on the comfortable sofa seat and have delicious meals in the cinema.  The food we've tried over the years has always been above average.

EagleWings is also incredibly convenient.  All the theaters are on level 1, and if you're looking to book a private function, you can head to level 2.

We highly recommend EagleWings Cinematics to anyone looking for a luxurious and memorable cinema experience.  It's a great option for a romantic date night, a family outing, or a night out with friends.

Golden Village is a reliable cinema option in the west, located conveniently within Jurong Point near Boon Lay MRT. The cineplex has multiple screening halls with comfortable seating and good screen and sound quality, making it suitable for regular movie outings.

Ticketing is efficient with self-service kiosks and automated entry gates, which helps reduce waiting time during peak hours. The cinema typically screens a wide range of movies, from mainstream releases to popular blockbusters.

The vibe is casual and family-friendly, though it can get bustling during weekends and evening shows. Being inside Jurong Point also means easy access to food outlets and shops before or after your movie.

A practical choice if you’re looking for a straightforward and comfortable cinema experience in the Jurong area. 🎬🍿

Watched hoppers last night, last showtime of the day. Ordered a mix of sweet and salted popcorn, but some of the popcorn tasted weird, like antiseptic. It did have a numbing sensation. And it was mostly from the left side of my popcorn box (not sure if this even matters). Thankfully we already read the other review before we visited, so we stopped ourselves from consuming the rest of the popcorn 💔💔 Never had such issues with other GV outlets… please QC!!

24 Nov 2025, Went for Jurong GV, 11am Wicked for Good. Delay for 39 mins, picture of not align, seeing blur movie, later got picture no music, later got movie no picture. Could not finish movie because of delay. What GV is trying to do? Look more like a scam... Need to report to CASE for such issue. It so irresponsible. Spoil the whole program for the family days. Some audience left.

At 12pm, the staff ask the rest of the audience whether they would like to move to another theater to see the movie properly.

Haven’t been to the cinema in forever and finally went to catch Zootopia 2 but the seats smells here (Theatre 4). Wonder if it’s because not much people watch movies these days so the hygiene level dropped?

3 stars cause seat is pretty comfy and there’s Ijooz now which you can bring into the theatre! It’s priced $1 higher than other machine tho. GV toilets v big and clean.

It was very disappointing this time. I have come to golden village a lot of times before but this has not happened before. There was this staff who shouted at me when I was buying the popcorn. We said sweet for the popcorn but ended up scooping salty. Hence we had to stop her and tell her it was sweet. However she had already done it so we had to get mixed or salty. And she shouted at us whether sweet or salty in a very disrespectful manner. In the end we had to get mixed. It was a very a disappointing experience.
Nice playground,  suggested for kids above 6yo.
Tengah has amazing playgrounds with tall big tunnel slides - perfect for 5 years and above! This is one of the best in Singapore! It is next to the Tengah Bus Interchange.

Visited in March 2024.

The abandoned park is fairly dangerous in certain areas, particularly the famed Torii boardwalk, which is a great way to cross from the human world to the land of the kami in a permanent way with a wrong step. Fallen trees have crushed the boardwalk and obstructed it at the same time, so a visitor cannot go to the other end of the park without exiting or navigating the steep slopes. I also saw bees as I was coming out, 20 or so, on an artificial rock near the stone step entrance.

The abandoned kampong plantation behind the Hillside park at BB West Ave 5 is much more interesting in my opinion. There was the ruins of what likely was the main house with a dried up well, a big circular open water tank and another rectangular water tank. The 2 water tanks were located behind the house where a small stream, likely a former longkang, supplied water to the circular water tank. This circular tank had 1 or 2 catfish inside, which may explain the clear water and relative lack of mosquitoes. Behind the circular tank is a rectangular water tank that likely used to rear fish, but has since cracked after being abandoned. It is very tranquil there, and is certainly worth a short visit, as it is not too difficult to reach.

Do be aware of wild boars and snakes, I was alerted by a pair of Bangladeshi workers that a snake dropped in front of me, which I failed to notice. They also mentioned seeing wild boars and their digging activities, so watch out. Oh, also got pandan growing there.

Both Hillside Park 1 and 2 are essential climate control mechanism which are highly beneficial to the health of residents within a 1km radius from its location. We must protect Hill 2 from being exploited by HDB (which it did to Hill 1). Hill 2 actually lowers the ambient temperature by up to 3 deg C across the Bukit Batok Polyclinic.
Challenging path for beginners like me... luckily experience hikers tied pink and yellow ribbon on trees... Adventures journey to the top... Jiayoh, you also can do it to find the Torri Singapore

Fell onto a thorn bush, stepped on an ant nest, bitten by mosquitoes, slipped and landed on my butt, friend walked into a spider web with a humongous spider! Want more could you ask for from an adventure! Go from the difficult entrance and be rewarded by the shrine. Be ready to track back and try again and again. Practise your gut-feel and navigation skills.
First time I've come here is very interesting places ,can see the Tori gate and others many things you can find here, good for who love tracking n hiking.
Many birds and nice places to explore  nature. About 2km+ with proper walking path. Must bring insects repellent, many many mosquitos
Kranji gate is better reached by cycling or driving as roads do not have sidewalks

Super quaint and ulu place where most of the visitors are bird watchers (run into a few during the visit). Quite hard to reach because closest bus stops (let alone train stations) are kilometers away. Walking to the location is also hard since the road has no pavement. The upside is that it's free of charge and the observation tower at the end of the trail gives a nice vantage point over the bird reserve and Kranji Reservoir nearby. Maintenance works could've been better since some of the sheds seem to be quite run down.
Very quiet place with few visitors. Smell of animal poo, probably some animal farm nearby.

Kranji Marshes offers a unique escape into nature, but it's a mixed bag in terms of maintenance and experience. Located in Singapore, the marshes are a good option for those looking to immerse themselves in a quieter, more natural environment away from the hustle and bustle of the city.

The walking trail is decent, making for a pleasant trek, and I found that a 30-minute walk provided an opportunity to appreciate the surrounding flora and fauna. However, the lack of upkeep was evident. Some paths were overgrown, and the area felt somewhat neglected, which detracted from the overall experience.

One significant downside was the mosquito situation. I ended up with 18 bites during my visit, so I recommend bringing insect repellent or wear long sleeves and pants if you decide to go. The marshes can feel quite deserted, which might appeal to some seeking solitude, but it could also be a bit unsettling for others.

Overall, Kranji Marshes has potential for a refreshing nature walk, but be prepared for a few inconveniences along the way. If you are willing to overlook the maintenance issues and the pesky mosquitoes, it can be a worthwhile retreat into Singapore's natural landscape.

Rifle Range Nature Park is a great extension to the Bukit Timah nature area, offering a mix of easy trails, lush rainforest scenery, and interesting glimpses into Singapore’s natural and historical heritage. The park is well connected, with clearly marked paths, boardwalks, and links to nearby nature reserves

One unexpected highlight was spotting a durian tree - and even seeing fallen durians on the ground, a reminder of how rich and diverse the forest ecosystem is. Along the trails, you’ll also find impressive giant trees, dense vegetation, quarry views, and remnants of the area’s past hidden among the greenery

I loved Rifle Range Nature Park — it’s very friendly for children and the elderly. The boardwalks are well-paved, anti-slip, and mostly barrier-free, so it’s easy for prams and seniors to walk comfortably.

The access is super convenient: just take Beauty World MRT Exit A, go to Level 4 of Beauty World Centre, and cross the overhead bridge that leads directly into the park. Highlights include the Quarry Wetland, Colugo Deck, and a fun Forest Exploration Trail for kids. Highly recommended for a relaxed family nature walk.

Nice new park with natural trails and paved paths. Has many unspoilt flora and fauna, saw several durian and starfruit trees along the paths. Not so easy to get to if depending on public transport. Toilets and carpark are available and some paths r equipped with CCTV! Not crowded on a weekday morning.

A nice trek away from the hustle and bustle of the city located in the Bukit Timah area of Singapore. Amidst a lush green rain forest with thriving ecosystems Easy to access and to reach on foot and public transport from the nearest MRT station called Beauty World - Downtown Line (Blue Coloured Line). An overhead bridge links one of the entrances of the park to the roof top Food Court (highest floor) of Beauty World Shopping Centre. Easy enough for amateur trekkers, family outings, a stroll in the park or a jog. There is a main building for meeting area, car drop off and pick up, giant ceiling fans to cool you down, chilled drinking water dispenser, vending machines, maps, loo and a park ranger on duty.  Good place to shelter if it rains There are some slopes on the way up to the cliff top of the old quarry with a stunning viewing Birds Eye area perched right at the top of the cliff. Not a long distance but short slightly steep slopes but totally worth the climb. Saw families with kids around the age of 5 and above at the top so it’s really not that challenging or difficult. Also Not as far as I expected. But do take note of your way back because if you take a wrong turn and end up on Rifle Range Road it’s more than a 1 hour walk to the nearest MRT station especially if you are already tired. However the paths are all well paved and safe. Do note the park closes at 7pm. You can also access this park from the Green Corridor.

About mid range level of difficulty. Lot of monkey though. Place is very peaceful and the lookout area is scenic. In awe. It’s connected to Rail Corridor as well, that one is pretty easy in difficulty level. It’s a literally straight road. Just need endurance for the distance.
The pond with all the turtles on the other side of the building is really nice.

Silver Garden at HortPark is one of the best highlights of this peaceful green space in Alexandra. Known for its striking silver, grey, and pale-green foliage, it offers a visually refreshing experience. A notable feature is the polished metal artwork displayed among the greenery, adding a modern, artistic contrast to the garden’s natural surroundings. A great spot for a leisurely stroll or photography—definitely worth visiting when exploring HortPark.
From far away, really looks like frosted silvery leaves with whitish hues which was a delightful surprise since we're in tropical climate
Nice garden inside Hort Park. Interesting plants and flowers to see. Good to see the greenery in the middle of huge buildings
Amazing walk in the beautiful place

​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.
Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

🚢Battleship Playground is a sand-filled Children Playground.
🛳️The Playground features a beautiful battleship design play structure centrepiece.
⚓The design is inspired by the Park's past as a Naval Base.
🚢The play structure features slides, bridges, and climbing elements.
⛴️Separately, there is also a Swing set and a Climb Net.
⏰Children can spent hours here having a great time.
🥤Drinking water fountain is located close by.
🚻And the public toilet is also near to the Playground.
🚢Battleship Playground is a great place for the children to explore and have lots of fun.

A battleship playground jn semabangwang park. Has many climbing nets and 2 slides. Very sandy area so best to wear covered shoes. The swing is filled with sand so it can be a bit uncomfortable using the swing. But is has great places for children to play catching or hide and seek
Great playground, novel design.  Do wish it was a bit better maintained, but a great stop on an evening walk nonetheless.

The playground look old, but the battle ship still function well.

If bring kids here need to be extra careful as there have monkeys around n they’re not afraid of u, I guess have ppls feed them that’s why they will come towards u.
swings were a 👎🏼 but the room upstairs was a 👍🏼to smoke & lepak with cool designs & pole for adult fun🤭good slides but a little small for bigger kids (adults like us).
Probably one of the best BTOs ever in Tengah Garden District. Looking forward to its completion!
Surrounding all roads still closed. Wonder how are we going to arrange renovation after getting our keys this month.

Kallang Park Connector is an absolute gem for anyone who appreciates a bit of wilderness in the heart of the city. As a regular spot for jogging, it offers much more than just a paved path; the air is incredibly fresh, making those morning or evening runs feel revitalizing. What really sets this route apart, though, is the incredible biodiversity. During my visits, I’ve encountered everything from vibrant insects to the occasional snakes in the river—nature is definitely thriving here.

For those who find themselves constantly looking toward the water, the sightings of various fish species are a fantastic bonus that adds to the charm of the area. It’s the perfect blend of a functional fitness route and a mini-safari. Whether you're working on your cardio or just looking to clear your head, the connection to nature here is top-notch. If you're looking for an all-in-one spot to stay active and enjoy the outdoors, this is the place to be. Highly recommended for nature enthusiasts and fitness lovers alike!

Kallang Park Connector is a linear green trail in Singapore’s Park Connector Network (PCN) that runs along the Kallang River and links Bishan-Ang Mo Kio Park in the north to Kallang Riverside Park in the south.

It’s about 7.87 km long and provides a scenic route for walking, jogging, and cycling through several central residential areas

We definitely enjoyed our walk along the Kallang PCN.

There's a weird sign pointing towards bishan park (the wrong way), confused us for a moment.

One of the most scenic park connector in Singapore linking Clark Quay and City center to Kallang, Kallang Bahru, Potong Pasir and all the way to Bishan Park. Perfect for cycling, jogging and brisk walking.
Very good area for relax your mind

The 2 stretches of Kallang Park connector that go under the bridges are finally open this week! Lovely artwork. Kudos to the artists that put them up and pat on the back for the workers whp were involved in the construction.  Joggers and cyclists can now bypass 2 traffic lights at Kallang Bahru and Upper Boon Keng Road, to get from Bendemeer Road to Kallang MRT. Celebration!

It was a breezy morning on December 8, so we decided to take a walk along the Alexandra Park Connector again. The canal was exceptionally clean, beautifully reflecting the surroundings like a mirror. Kudos to the dedicated groundskeepers for maintaining such a pristine environment and keeping Singapore the clean city it’s known to be. Appreciation also to the Friends of the Park for their continuous efforts in upkeeping this lovely space.
Nice pathway for walks - saw several flora and fauna along the way.

It was a nice walk from Queenstown MRT to Robertson Quay via the Alexandra Park Connector. The stretch of Alexandra Park Connector is not long, and the portion starting from the condominium The Crest is very scenic next to the Singapore River. The Park Connector was not crowded and we had good weather resulting in a pleasant and easy walk which took slightly more than an hour

Wonderful jogging 🏃‍♂️ and cycling 🚴‍♂️ path along the canal and river; a picturesque path to get from Redhill to CBD along the Singapore River.

At the end of the Redhill Tanglin Road end, there is a tranquil park built on top of the canal and is a great place for personal space exercises or reading a book.

If you need food & beverage, there are many options throughout the several exits.

Alexandra Park Connector connect to Queenstown on the West and all the way to Clarke Quay on the East. Along the way you will enjoy the green scenery of the florals and fauna’s as well as some modern skyscrapers. This way can also lead you to Gardens by the Bay and all the way to East Coast Park.

We are very happy with the nursery school and the care provided by the teachers. The teachers are kind, patient, and supportive. Our child enjoys going to school and has shown good progress in communication, social interaction, and confidence.

The school provides a safe, nurturing, and engaging environment for children to learn and grow. We appreciate the regular updates and the dedication of the teaching staff. Thank you for helping our child develop and enjoy learning.

We highly recommend this nursery school to other parents.

I would give 10 stars if I could!!! My son started N1 this year and he is having such a fun time in school. Special shoutout to his teachers (teacher Ping 游老师) who make school so fun and welcoming for him. Thankful for their dedication and genuine care for my son with regular updates via the app/ answering my concerns etc. All the teachers at drop off are really friendly as well, which makes drop offs much easier! The admin staff (Bao) is super friendly, replies promptly and made sure I signed all the relevant documents correctly. Thank you teachers for your care, hopefully you can have lesser admin work to do!!

S4K has been a wonderful school for my child. This  can be seen through his behaviour whenever he go to school. The teachers are caring and patient, and they make sure my child feels safe and happy. There are curriculum for your kids in school which can enhance their learning. The environment is friendly and welcoming, which helps my child feel comfortable and excited to go to school. Happy that I have enrolled my child to S4K!

We’re truly grateful for the care and dedication shown by the infant care team. From the start, the teachers have been warm, patient, and attentive, making the transition much easier for both our baby and us as parents. Knowing that our child is cared for in a clean, safe, and nurturing environment gives us great peace of mind each day. We really appreciate the regular updates and the genuine love the team shows to every baby. Thank you for taking such wonderful care of our little one. ❤️

I highly recommend S4K Tengah Garden Vale. The teachers there have been dedicated in guiding my children. They always updates you on your child development & milestones and will continue to work alongside with parents for the children to do well.

After 15 years, NJC's boarding school (only) was officially closed in 2024 to return the land for state use.

These images hope to honour the memories of those 500 students and teachers boarded there every year

For more at #shuutur

Very disappointing. The secondary level teachers dont really teach. I was expecting some principles to be taught in class so I kept quiet and observed my kid's progress. Wasn't taught. Disappointed. They also got 1 alumni to teach while waiting for her A results. Teachers not teaching in way that can be understood. The school relies on private tuition and hard self study. A few teachers talks disrespectful like are sarcastic. Student prefers the previous principal

One of the famous schools in Singapore and undoubtedly with lots of heritage, history and culture. As you step into the school you feel the nostalgia and history working together to build a wonderful, hopeful and resilient new generation one after the other.

Shoes and shoelaces that are garish in nature are not allowed. W school, W people, W teachers, and most importantly, W chillies, popcorn and math.

Everyday I go to school and I look forward to seeing my math work. The piles and piles of math homework does not do itself. It sure is great experience and practice!!!

The accidental cohesive nature of this school is truly great. All that gleams is not gold. The equal funding given to the aesthetics and science departments are truly interesting.

I recall the days I went to NJC. Oh my, it has been quite a bit... I can recall the days we got our exam results back. I remember the smiles our teacher tried to hide from the students when they gave us those earth-shatteringly wonderful results. They smiled so wide, so wide we could barely see the tears forming in their eyes as they looked at our shattered expressions in pride.

The teachers love teaching, sometimes a little too much. I see their beautiful teary-eyes smiles upon their weary faces whenever they step into class. I see it, I feel it, I know it, it is ingrained into each and every one of their fabric of reality. As an alumni of this school, I missed the days of boarding. The chaotic thwacking of doors whenever we closed a door was truly inspiring.

The myths are not true. We do not have a leaky roof. In fact, it is the best roofs of all. The roof's whole is about winning. In fact, it is so winnable that it has always allowed water through it. It has never lost its purpose in letting water through.

One of the key problems today is that math is undervalued in this society, and that there is too much emphasis on the pursuit of arts. What even is the use for arts? Useless pining? Useless uselessness? Useless emotions? None of these things are needed. Join NJC, for you will see the world for what it is, through good, or through better, or through best, and that, is through math.

Our integrated programme is good. No, the best at INTEGRATING!!!! Our streak of streaky teary students crying tears of joy at the sight of their beautiful eggs received on their math papers is unmatched. One must join this institution to truly understand the meaning of life. For it is to MATH.

I truly apologise for this accidental cohesive multicultural heritage-ridden serviced and honoured post. NJCians truly deserve more math homework.

Teachers are unable to teach. You need to engage tutors to get your child to understand the material. They overload the students with homework and you pray that your child survives this barrage of tests, the humiliation of failing and the lack of proper help to improve. Don't send your child here unless he/she is prepared to weather the storm and you are prepared for the many teacher/parent sessions.

As a part-time software engineer student currently in my second semester, I am happy to share my experience regarding the campus layout and accessibility. Navigating a new campus can be tricky, so here is a simple guide to reaching the School of Computing.

Getting to Campus
The campus is very convenient to access via public transport:
• MRT Station: Take the East-West Line to Dover MRT.
• Exit: Use Exit A to leave the station.
• Connectivity: Once you exit, you will find a skybridge that conveniently links the different blocks together, keeping you sheltered from the weather.

Navigating to the School of Computing
For fellow part-time students arriving for evening or weekend classes, please follow these simple steps:
1. Entry: Proceed through the visitor gantry at the main entrance.
2. Wayfinding: Look for the directional signage posted along the walkways.
3. Location: Follow the tags leading toward the School of Computing.
4. Specific Block: The department is located at Block T21.
5. Floor: Take the lift or stairs up to the 4th Floor.

I know this place well. Located at the western tip of the campus and nearest point of SP to Clementi. Nearest vehicular entrance is Gate3 and nearest exit is Gate7. The School used to be the Japan-Singapore Institute of Software Technology. Now it is sharing the site with another school in SP. Food Court 6 on its 2nd floor and the BizIT library is just round the corner..
The university contains a huge campus and the campus is amazing.
One of the most iconic architecture building by Thomas Heatherwick in Singapore! The texture used and the orientation of the organic building were impressive

Smart Campus Experience: Safety & Tech Done Right!

"This isn't just a campus - it's a tech-powered campuss. I was blown away by the thousands of CCTV cameras (yes, you read that right!) with over 1,000  analytics cameras constantly safeguarding students. Forget old-school security - this is next-level protection!

Parking is frictionless genius: barrier-free entry, EV charging stations, and the game-changing Go Parkin app.

Register your vehicle once, and the automatic plate scanners handle everything - no tickets, no cash, just drive in/out while the app bills you seamlessly.

Between the Instagram-worthy landmarks (seriously, bring your camera!) and seeing how every detail prioritizes safety and convenience...

If you want peace of mind with 21st-century techno education, then this campus sets the gold standard.

Nanyang Technological University (NTU), sprawling across its lush, eco-friendly sanctuary at 50 Nanyang Avenue, is a breathtaking masterclass in how a modern research institution can harmonize high-octane innovation with environmental stewardship in 2026. From my deeply insightful exchanges with pioneering materials scientists and AI developers who call this campus home, it is impossible not to be captivated by NTU’s evolution into a high-fidelity, merytorical powerhouse of global renown. My colleagues frequently marvel at the pedagogical electricity within "The Hive" and "The Arc," describing a rare environment where scholars are empowered to master complex sustainable energy or aerospace systems through a uniquely collaborative, cross-disciplinary lens. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, positioning the university not just as a technical school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that is currently dominating the fields of green chemistry, robotics, and digital humanities. Reflecting on these glowing, authentic testimonials from peers who have revolutionized global supply chains, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of scientific audacity and garden-city grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "Smart Campus" initiative, which my associates describe as a living laboratory where the future of urban mobility and carbon neutrality is being forged in real-time. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring researcher as a vital partner in the quest for global solutions. It is truly heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the university’s commitment to "NTU 2025/2026" strategic goals has created a resilient, future-ready workforce that remains peerless in the international labor market. Many of my professional contacts highlight that the university’s robust industry partnerships provide a distinct competitive edge, ensuring that theoretical breakthroughs are rapidly translated into tangible societal benefits. I personally admire the way NTU maintains a respectful dialogue with its global counterparts while fearlessly pushing the boundaries of what is possible in the Fourth Industrial Revolution. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces the belief that it is an extraordinary engine of both intellectual and economic transformation. The school’s focus on the "Triple Helix" of academia, industry, and government is, in my view, the definitive blueprint for 21st-century educational success. I find it remarkable how the campus provides such a serene, professional backdrop that simultaneously inspires deep focus and radical creativity. Those who have navigated its rigorous, world-class curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful curiosity is meticulously refined into elite professional expertise. Ultimately, aligning one’s academic trajectory with Nanyang Technological University is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for any ambitious individual determined to lead the global technological transition while remaining part of a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. This institution stands as a brilliant testament to Singapore’s unwavering dedication to the highest calibers of scientific and humanistic excellence.

Really big and nice campus. There are some distinct architecture there. It's a bit hard to get to, but I think the distance effects how big the campus is. I didn't study there, I just visited so I'm not sure about the education though.

Jia xin is one of the best tutors ive ever had!! My bio was super bad in j1 and i could only get like at most 30/100 💀

But then i started tuition with her and she was rlly patient and actually listened to identify my areas of weaknesses. She gives concise notes + answering techniques + correctly targets important topics commonly tested. The discussion during lessons and explaining concepts is also incredibly effective

Aside from that she’s also super chill and fun to talk to! At the end thanks to her help i got A for A levels!!😝

heh ok coach jia xin has been teaching me for 5 years (since p5 heh 😼) n highkey shes one of the best tuition teachers ive had 😋😋 vv easygoing, patient n fun to talk to !

i look forward to her lessons (e math n a math heh) n her explanations r easy to understand and digest,, her late night lessons r rly good as well n she tries her best to fit into ur timeslot for said lessons !
during exam period, she writes handwritten letters to encourage her students,, those said letters r what push me thru that tough period 🫶

wo her help id lwky be failing by now ok but ive been faring well in my studies n its because of her ! and even if u dont do well, she wont scold u but instead talk u thru what happened, how u can improve and how she as a tuition teacher can help u out !!

if ure looking for a teacher that can help u w math and sci, id 101 percent recommend her ! not only is she an excellent tchr who explains things rly well but shes also a very friendly person to talk to !

I still remember back when my daughter was in primary 5... We tried a few different math tutors, but she always told me that they became impatient or that she didn’t really understand their explanations. Then a friend referred me to Teacher Jia Xin.

After the first lesson with her, my daughter told me it was fun, clear, and that she didn’t feel stressed asking questions when she didn’t understand something. We decided to continue with her...and 3 years later, both my children are now in secondary school and still with her for math and sci.

Thank you Teacher Jia Xin. We really appreciate the patience and guidance you have given my kids 🙏

This teacher actually carried my math results I don't think I could ever have understood math without her. She actually goes through everything and makes sure I understand the thought process behind the methods to solve the equations !! She's also super fun to talk to and I look forward to each and every lesson 🎉 100% would reccomend

Jia Xin is a very patient, caring and kind tutor. Before attending her class, my son was unable to answer the Science OEQ but after attending her class, he is able to answer the questions well. You wont regret engaging her as your child's tutor.

To be fair,.coaches are approachable and committed. However, dealing with the admin is an extremely frustrating experience. Was told in mid April that my weekend classes will all be conducted during weekdays(4 days in a row) for the June holidays. Feedback that timeline is too short to make arrangements. As working parents, there is a reason why we send for weekend classes. Not realistic to expect that we take leave for 4 days to send kids for class. Instead of addressing my concerns, admin just kept offering dates and even sending a receipt for payment made to me. Feedback that perhaps if there are such special arrangements, to provide schedule way in advance or provide 2 week break for the holidays since they don't offer credits. Again, ignored.
Enjoyed the art and chemistry workshop. My son had lots of fun.
Coach Siew fang is very nice and attentive and create lots of fun times with the kids

instic is a great tution centre the coaches are super nice you should definitely join instic i love math because of instic
i ❤️ instic i am instic's number one fan

We have had an excellent experience with Instic. The teachers are incredibly dedicated and committed to helping my child succeed. Thanks to their support and guidance, my child achieved his academic goals. I highly recommend this tuition centre for anyone looking to provide their child with top-notch educational support!

Internet, chatgbt, google, Ai and the pervasive mobile devices may have changed the way we seek answers. But a library is where serious learnings and discoveries are found.

They are depository of knowledge, curated and preserved. The Central Library is that - and more.

There are librarians dedicated to the preservation of knowledge and their proliferation. Here, they are not stuffy and grumpy matrons. They are professionals and helpful in a way we boomers never knew.

Tell them a hypothesis and they can help you turn an idea into blueprint. Need to fill a gap in citations, they can find you a landfill.

History is our heritage. Lessons learned to inform us. Latest technology is flashy but libraries tell us how they came about.

The Central Library is more than a meeting place for varsity students. Here is where a thousand nuggets of wisdom and knowledge exists - waiting for those with inquiring minds to be transformed into “professors.”

lots of study spots! most of the library is for nus students/staff only as there are gantries at the entrance which mainly open with student/staff passes

most study spots have charging spots, each level has a bathroom and water dispensers. there's also food options at level 1 and a bookstore with essentials

only complaint is that the wifi can be quite slow/spotty at times. also level 6 study area can get quite warm sometimes when its busy, like the ac isnt strong enough :")))

I spend a lot of time in this library when I did my undergrad and post grad studies. It has many books which were not available in other libraries. I did join as a corporate member for a few years too.
Beautiful and modern library with a very rich collection of books and ample seating space.

I entered as an alumnus. I needed an NUS alumnus lifelong email address and also had to download and install the UNivUS app, which generates the QR code for me to enter the library. There was mobile data signal at the registration counter therefore I needed to log into NUG Guest wifi. But the NUS Guest wifi needed an OTP which required mobile signal! Therefore I needed to go back and forth between the Forum (mobile signal to get OTP) and library registration desk (for wifi). Altogether, it took me perhaps half an hour to sort it a out and finally enter the library. Future trips should be straightforward I hope!!! General stacks on 5th floor. Photocopier is on 5th floor but in the Chinese SEA Collection. Use your EZ-link card. $0.03 per A4 - great value!  :-)  The facilities are lovely -wow... compared to 1990s! Love the place. The Coop had lots of cute merch too. Ah... to be a Gen Z or Gen Alpha... 😄😄😄
MRT station for NUH. For NUS, you will need to walk a little or take bus. The station is quite deep and the escalator up is very long.

Muhammad fairuz is worth complimenting. I was feeling breathless, he wheelchair me down and even call the bishan circle line to assist met when I reached there. Such kindness is heart warming. Nuh said they don't have conceige . He deserves promotion.

Drink already ready.. juat sit at it while making other things and make customer wait. Am the only customer at that time. Then came another customer, instead of giving the ready drink, she serve another customer. And never use hand glove while making drink. Her colleague using hand glove except for her.
Well connected to NUH and NUS via the medical blocks. The station has plenty of amenities for whoever needing a bite before heading further towards the buildings.

Visited Geneo Mall and went to Kent Ridge MRT Station around 4pm (30/7/25). We were looking at the MRT map wondering where should we head to next. MRT staff Mr Ang approached us (he must have noticed us standing there for quite a while) and asked if we needed help. He was very pleasant and helpful. We asked him if there are any recommended places along the Circle Line suitable for elderlies (like us). Mr Ang was kind enough to suggest that Harbourfront Station is only a few stations away and it is connected to a wide array of retail and F&B options. He even suggested it is possible to take a stroll to Sentosa nearer to the evening when ‘is not too hot’, via the sheltered Boardwalk. Appreciate Mr Ang’s thoughtfulness, positive attitude, proactive assistance and high level of service!

One-North is a very well-structured MRT station, perfectly servicing one of Singapore's major business and industrial districts. The layout is refreshingly straightforward, making it a station where you're unlikely to ever be late due to navigation issues. With multiple exits that integrate directly into the surrounding buildings, it’s a 'get the job done' kind of stop that feels custom-built for the busy professionals working in the area.

Was a nice station, until the recent changes for the next 3 months:
1. Crowd control that led to the need to take a longer route, even when the crowd is not that bad
2. Lower frequency
3. Mrt stops between stations for some time
You would love the nice aroma when you come out of the train in the morning. Still can't figure out from which store. A lot of good food options. Cold storage at the MRT level.

🟧One-north MRT Station
🟨 9 Ayer Rajah Ave, Singapore 138647

🟦One-North Station is connected to the integrated office/retail complexes of Fusionopolis, Connexis, Symbiosis and Biopolis and other office towers.

🟧 The Insead Business School is in close proximity to the One-North station.
New, clean, convenient access MRT station which connects to the circle line (yellow line). There are more and more restaurants, bars  opened nearby, you could also find stationary shop and clinic.
Will be useful for those travelling from Tengah to Jurong East when it completes (currently planned for 2028).
Until this Jurong region line gets connected to alternative line like circle, The bottle neck at EWL is still going to be a strain for commuters in the west.
It's ok

place_name: 20 Ghim Moh Road Market & Food Centre
review_text: Another addition to the list of over-rated food centres in Singapore. A rather large food centre, but food is not up to par. My taste buds have yet to be pleasured by any of the offerings here. No matter how "good" some people claim certain stalls are, I am yet to be impressed. When that changes, I will edit my review. But for now, this review will be sticking around.

place_name: 20 Ghim Moh Road Market & Food Centre
review_text:
I like to suggest that Ghim Moh food n hawker centre is a good or better places to go during breakfast n lunch
Has a nice variety of delicious hawker food congregated under one roof
Ample paid parking lots.
Next block has Aircond shop to buy fresh fruits n vegetables till 10 pm at night.
Can find  stalls selling yummy food:
1.Whampoa soya bean drinks
2.Delisnacks- good Yu tiao, butterfly bun, curry puff etc( hve good oil to fry)
3.Tong Fong Fatt Hainanese boneless chicken rice( chicken is smooth, young n tender with good soup)
4.Chuan Kee duck rice
5.Thye Hong Handmade fishball noodles
6..Ghim Moh carrot cake
7.Jiu Jiang Shao la roast meats
8.Teck Hin Chee Cheong fan( fantastic taste)
9.Hock Soon carrot cake
10.Ghim Moh Chwee kueh
11.Margaret Drive Hokkien fried prawn mee
12. Muhammed Faisal roti Prata ( nice prate n curries)
13. Hin fried hor fun

LATEST as of SUNDAY FIRST MARCH 2026
If you like curry puffs, there’s a new stall at Ghim Moh Market & Hawker Centre, located next to the famous homemade fishball noodle stall( Thye hong).

They sell the flaky type of curry puffs with a variety of fillings such as sardine, yam and curry chicken with potato. I tried the chicken curry potato version yesterday morning.

The skin was crispy and nicely flaky, and not overly oily — that’s a plus. However, the curry chicken filling was only average and did not really stand out. It lacked fragrance and depth of flavour.

Overall, I would rate it as average for now.

⭐⭐⭐ (3/5)

place_name: 20 Ghim Moh Road Market & Food Centre
review_text: Found this chai fun store at the coffee shop with 40 dishes and friendly staff. At 10AM, all the dishes were out, hot and fresh!!

place_name: 20 Ghim Moh Road Market & Food Centre
review_text: Amazingly fresh vegetables & all proteins, best organic chicken, fish and seafood. Was great to see they almost all speak English - incredible value! A must see for expats wanting to cook with fresh ingredients and hawker has some stunning classics & Michelin star

place_name: 20 Ghim Moh Road Market & Food Centre
review_text: Local authentic hawker food simple and delicious and affordable. Old time favorite black carrot cake extra sweet was best ..wash down with a cuppa Kopi O kosong . Feel 40% full..Q for another 15 mins chee chong fun ..the smooth texture taste of the chee cheong fun blend so well with tasty brown sauce and the generous sprinkle of sesame  seed. The chui kueh is a bit lack down..texture doesn't taste the same before  and chye por so little ..prefer Bedok interchange chui kueh.

This is one of the very good fish soup stall if u are in the west coast/ Clementi area.  Their fish is fresh, and they are not over-cooked.  Hence fish slices having a succulent texture.
Their next gem is the large minced pork ball.  Apart fm the golf ball size, they are also very flavorful.. (becos the minced pork is marinated with fine dried flounder fish powder, giving a rich umami flavor).

Quite similar to Grouper King’s minced pork ball.

Will be back!

Don't worry about quality, we have quantity, best sums up West Coast Market Square. Truth to be told, if not for the fact that I have to go to Inchcape Centre for car servicing, I would not step foot here. While there are many stalls and food options are readily available, the quality of food here leaves more to be desired. Characteristically of all large food centres, many stalls here sells the same items, and repetition is common place. After eating at 7-8 stalls, the only decent stall is the duck rice stall. All the other stalls cannot make it. Sorry to say, the place is over rated. Both vegetarian, noodle stalls serve mediocre food. It must be tough for the residents there to live with such a mediocre food centre. Give this place a miss. Not worth travelling here for a visit.
This econ rice is so good. Huge variety, tastes good, super well priced. $3.5 for my eggplant, tomato egg, and meatballs.

Great food here. A place where we can get authentic traditional hawker food. Like coming here, good food, good vide to get a sense of the old laid back Singapore feel. There are also many sundry and food supplies around the market. A great place to be.

$3 a plate of fried oyster! I took a second look at the price. Yes, $3,$4,$5. One of the most economical fried oyster you can get. Many food stalls here. Not to bad, but i prefer those that are crispy rather than starchy. He has a mix of both, i would say great for a quick fix, small plate $3 for teatime! Opp west coast plaza.  Cash only. #01-187

This place is terrible. Only thing worth eating is Yuen Kee, but even then the viral mixed sauce was just heaps of chilli oil….

Don’t order the salted egg rice from this store. For $7.80, portion was really little and I’d be ok if the salted egg sauce was rich but it’s extremely watered down…

Came on 1 May at around 7pm. There are still some seats around. Ordered chicken ramen from Korean-Japanese stall and Shrimp and vegetable dumplings (viral mixed sauce oil) and brocolli without garlic from Yuen Kee Dumpling store. Took around 10min for my food to be ready - was quite prompt, and they were able to customise my brocolli to exclude garlic. Taste was okay.
Beautifully designed, new, clean & spacious food court. Lots of different cuisines and varieties to choose from. Awesome place to chillax.
Happy to see my favourite stalls are here, drinks are good too. Koufu is great, can be better with more stalls? Yes please.

Above average prices for average to below average taste. Only the environment is good with functional air conditoning and the tables were spaced nicely apart. However, not sure why they made the tray return counter that small. A few trays and it is already full.

$6 for 炸酱面.

A bit pricey for the quantity of the ingredients given. The place is with air-conditioning, but it makes no sense that the price is the same as shopping malls at JE.

Taste is ok, if not, it would be 1 star.

_______________

Japanese cuisine

$6.80 chicken and salmon

Taste okay, but for the salmon... I think the tissue paper is thicker than the salmon.

The banmian store is actually so unhygienic and disgusting. I found an insect in my food multiple times, and this isn't the first time. The first time, I found a green caterpillar-looking insect in the food, but didn't take a photo. This time, I found this. And the aunty there is really rude as well. I spent $5.10 for this, and my food came with extra protein that I didn't ask for. Can something be done about the food quality and hygiene?

The koufu outlet is very clean, the staff are friendly and hardworking and attentive. Every slight drip of sauce or spillage is attended to immediately. They also have the safety of all their customers at the top of their mind. If anyone spills any liquid on the floor they will quickly put up the danger sign to warn the customers of spilled floor and to be careful.

Nice and clean food court at Plantation Plaza with a good variety of food options. The seating area is spacious and well-maintained. It’s a convenient spot for nearby residents to grab a quick and tasty meal.

The food here is a tragedy. I tried the newly opened Wok Delights hoping to finally taste some wok in the wokless Tengah, only to have my mouth violated by the Hor Fun. It did not taste like Hor Fun. Every bite has a mouthful of raw chopped garlic. The soup base tasted like 70% soy sauce, 30% sugar and 0% wok. There were only 2 pathetic shrimps the size of my toenail inside, and a few paper-thin slices of fish cake, yet costed 7.30💩

Newly opened Grounds on a Hill at Sunset Way is a clean, minimal, coffee-first space. Bright and airy inside, with the smell of fresh coffee carrying through the room. Outdoor seating is breezy and comfortable, anchored by solid wood block benches. Menu is simple with items like sourdough bread, which I didn't sample this visit.

Both white and black coffees were excellent. The house blend leans fruity with gentle berry notes, well-balanced and not overly acidic.

this was my second time at grounds, I had a lovely chat over carefully made coffee, with smiles and a warm welcome by the team. I learnt that this was a family run business, with beans roasted fresh themselves.

this abode in sunset way provides that human touch we need, and a place to really rest and escape for a little!

Very chill cafe that is located further inside from the main road (though the area is relatively small anyways) - but very well worth the walk there as they do serve up great coffee in a cool minimalistic environment!

☕ another coffee that hits the benchmark, the plum flavour really did follow through in my iced white, and similarly for the hot drink as well - not the best of the best but that’s my benchmark for a 5 stars coffee, being able to taste the aroma from my drink

🍞 the sourdough was alright, pretty pricey for the portion though it was a decent snack to be had

Might return to try their tarts in future, and also their other beans too!

Excellent coffee in a very well-designed coffee space, that is dog friendly. It’s one of my favourite places to bring people to. Thrilled to see adventurous coffee businesses bringing specialty coffee to the heartlands and neighbourhoods. Great enterprise by Rio and his daughters Paris and Sydney.

So pleased to have found this coffee shop, a real treasure in the most unassuming of locations.

The philosophy is simple; fantastic coffee at affordable prices. The family that own and run this place have an endearing love of the coffee they make, and they keep it simple - two varieties of filter coffee (the Peruvian Geisha is phenomenal!) and two varieties of espresso, all roasted to suit. You can see the passion that goes into each cup that is carefully & beautifully crafted. A simple but quality food menu as well and that’s it!

There is relaxed indoor and outdoor seating and I all-too-easily managed to pass a couple of hours there with a good book. If you’re in Singapore and you love speciality coffee, don’t miss it!

Exceptionally a perfect getaway peaceful place!
The charming small cafe is located in the same building with primary school in Clementi Woods Park, a pleasant greenery peaceful place for hiking, exploring natures in the breeze morning and a delightful brunch.

The food exceeded my expectations. The savory waffle was absolutely delicious! The Egg Benedict had poached eggs cooked with bagel sesame, perfectly done.

The waffle was thick and chewy, satisfying in texture, and went perfectly with the toppings. The owner was creativity in the dishes and excellent coffee.💞☕️

Highly recommended to soak in a personalized and exceptional dining experience!💞

The café is run by one person, who puts great care into both the coffee and the food, and is very warm and welcoming to customers. Today, on her recommendation, I tried her homemade cookies with coffee—absolutely delicious. And, my breakfast was great too!
It’s located just down from the car park, next to a kindergarten, inside Woods Park. It’s a quiet and peaceful spot, perfect for relaxing and enjoying a nice cup of coffee.
I found a great spot today.

Quaint and cozy cafe tucked away in Clementi Woods! The owner is a 1-man show but she's very friendly and the food is delicious (see how I emptied the plate 😆) I ordered the grilled cheese and ham sandwich.
If you stay nearby it's worth making a trip down :) But note they are closed on weekends and otherwise open from 8am-4.30pm!

Auntie Doris is very sweet and caring. I went there with my parents and we had lengthy conversation and very good food. Not a second we felt like we are in a Cafe, but it was more like a home we were invited to have a breakfast in. Very silent atmosphere with soft music inside this play school pulled me back into my childhood.

A very cute and cozy cafe tucked away in the corner of a building next to a preschool! I went to grab lunch and study, and the lady was very kind and friendly. My friend had a similar experience where he went and ended up talking with the lady there for a long time. The grilled cheese and ham sandwich was delicious and I really enjoyed the atmosphere there. I wish I had more time to visit again!

*Nostalgic Comfort Elevated to Perfection*

'​Home by Enjoy' is an absolute masterclass in how to honor traditional, soul-warming flavors while elevating them for a modern dining audience. True to its name, the restaurant delivers an experience that feels deeply comforting and familiar, yet executed with the precision and flair of a top-tier kitchen. It's 'Home, Truly'.

​The Food
​The meal was an absolute parade of hits, showcasing a brilliant balance of textures and robust flavors.

1. ​Salted Vegetable Duck Soup: A spectacular opening act. The broth was deeply savory with a beautiful, clean tang from the salted vegetables, perfectly extracting the rich essence of the duck without a hint of greasiness.

2. ​Sliced Chicken with Bentong Ginger & Salted Fish: A triumph of aromatic cooking. The fiery, woody punch of the premium Bentong ginger paired beautifully with the sharp, umami depth of the salted fish, coating the tender slices of chicken in a rich, deeply satisfying glaze. The bentong ginger is legendary!

3. ​Minced Pork with Mushrooms & Water Chestnut: This dish nailed the texture. The pork was incredibly juicy and flavorful, interspersed with the earthy richness of mushrooms and the delightful, refreshing crunch of water chestnut. It is the ultimate elevated comfort food.

4. ​Vegetarian Truffle Cream Tofu: A brilliant modern twist. The silken, velvety tofu provided a delicate canvas for a luxurious, aromatic truffle cream that was rich but remarkably light, leaving a wonderful lingering fragrance.

​Ambience & Decor
​The decor is a thoughtful blend of contemporary chic and cozy nostalgia. It features stylish design elements and warm, inviting lighting that instantly makes you feel relaxed. The atmosphere is vibrant yet intimate, making it the perfect setting for lingering conversations over a spectacular meal. It feels premium, but entirely unpretentious.

​Service
​The service was exceptional and genuinely warm. The staff moved with a polished efficiency, ensuring dishes arrived hot and well-paced. Their hospitality felt authentic and attentive, mirroring the "home" philosophy of the restaurant by making us feel thoroughly looked after throughout lunch.

'​Home by Enjoy' takes local, comforting flavors and treats them with the absolute highest respect. If you want a meal that feeds the soul as much as the palate, this place is flawless. A must-visit!

This restaurant is tucked away in an office building in Bukit Merah. It was fairly full on a weekday evening, so it's advisable to make reservations in advance. The place has a retro 70s vibe. Ordered several dishes like oyster omelet, stewed lamb, pork knuckles, and stir-fried spicy eggplant. All the dishes were delicious especially the first two. The desserts were divine

We came on Friday evening and the place was packed. We were quickly brought to table when the diners were done. Ordered plates via the QR code and was quick to be served. Food was delicious. Oyster was big. The lala wasn’t my favourite just too much garlic and I don’t like the sweetness of Chili. Overall food was not bad.

clean and nice environment! beautiful decorations ✨ food was scrumptious as well, big portions and wide spread of food to choose from! can be a little on the pricier side but the taste and atmosphere covers for it! awesome customer service as well, uncle who attended us was friendly and proactive 😊 much thanks for the wonderful meal 🙂‍↕️🙏🏻

Second time back here. Cosy environment and superb service! The food is good 👍☺️ especially liked the oyster omelette - one of the best I’ve ever had! We tried the orh nee and it was pretty good too. Will recommend this place 👍

place_name: Abundance: Taiwanese Restaurant & Craft Beer Bar (Redhill)
review_text:
Was recommended this restaurant when I was searching for an eatery near central west and is MRT accessible. No regrets as the food was good but what stood out was the warm and genial service which made the meal outstanding for my friends and I.

Chose the fried chicken bao which I felt was filling but not outstanding although that's my personal opinion. Think the pork belly bao could be a better selection for me next time.

The beef noodles were excellent although I felt the soup was about too salty and not something I would drink on its own. The side dishes were nice and I loved that they served sparkling water in 850ml bottles for a few dollars!

Peanut ice-cream was nice and I would have it again definitely.

All in, a nice meal made excellent by great service!

place_name: Abundance: Taiwanese Restaurant & Craft Beer Bar (Redhill)
review_text:
I visited this place for lunch with my colleague to celebrate my birthday. We ordered the lunch set for two and added on the braised pork rice. As it was our first visit, we signed up via Oddle and managed to redeem a free drink as well.
The food was delicious and very well portioned. Everything was flavourful and generous — we were so full that we couldn’t even finish all the dishes.
What truly made the experience special was the service. The staff were incredibly sweet, especially Danny. When I mentioned that it was my birthday, he went the extra mile and surprised me with a bowl of 长寿面 topped with a perfectly runny sunny-side-up egg. And to my surprise they even came with candle!It was such a thoughtful gesture and absolutely perfect. Everyone end up took turn to wish me birthday which is super heart warming!
Overall, great food, generous portions, and heartfelt service. We really enjoyed our meal and will definitely be back again. Highly recommended!

place_name: Abundance: Taiwanese Restaurant & Craft Beer Bar (Redhill)
review_text:
1. Beef noodle: very tender beef. Decent portion. Rich and flavourful soup. Recommended.

2. Gua Bao: the pork belly was a little dry. I had high expectations due to good reviews. Not recommended.

The prices are on the higher side as prices on menu doesn't include gst and service charge.

First time member can redeem a free drink.

place_name: Abundance: Taiwanese Restaurant & Craft Beer Bar (Redhill)
review_text: Our 3rd return to Abundance in June 2026. Ordered a set for 4pax. Food had great variety incl. their famous peanut ice cream dessert. Every dish was delicious. Service was also great--they check on us to see if everything was okay. We had a pet dog so we had the outside table and they were accommodative to our needs. I highly recommend their set menus (for 2 or 4 pax, 49.90+, and 99.90+) and also make reservations. Always fully packed.

place_name: Abundance: Taiwanese Restaurant & Craft Beer Bar (Redhill)
review_text:
Had such a satisfying Taiwanese meal at Abundance @ Lengkok Bahru

My hubby absolutely loved the beef noodle soup — and that says a lot! The beef was incredibly soft and tender, almost melt-in-your-mouth, and the broth was rich, comforting, and full of depth. He was totally blown away and kept saying how good it was even after we left 😄

We also tried their gua bao, which was just as delightful. The bun was fluffy and light, hugging the savoury filling perfectly — a great balance of flavours and textures in every bite.

I went for the dumpling noodle, and it did not disappoint at all! The noodles were wonderfully chewy, with that wavy, springy texture. Love the freeze lychee tea drink!

The only downside is that the restaurant is small, best to make reservation during peak hours. But the food definitely makes up for it.

Overall, such a cosy spot for authentic Taiwanese comfort food. Definitely a place we’ll come back to when cravings hit again
I visited Plantation Plaza at Tengah and found it clean, modern, and convenient. There is a good mix of food and retail shops, making it a nice place for daily shopping and meals.
I love how this lively plaza shows up out of nowhere in ulu Tengah and serves thousands of residents. I saw CNY decoration but failed to spot the CNY Zodiac predictions though..
If you hungry , eat here 😋 price is slightly lower $5.50 per set , fried chicken set value for money

A convenient neighbourhood plaza with a nice mix of shops and eateries. You can find everything from groceries and food stalls to lifestyle stores. The place is clean and well-maintained — a great spot for residents nearby to shop or grab a quick meal.
Plenty of food choices here. But the parking at basement really messy. Don't know where is the nearest exit to the shopping mall. There is a big playground beside the mall.

Visited Cold Storage at around 6:55 PM on 1 June 2026 and purchased a loaf of sourdough bread. As the store has an automatic bread-slicing machine, I politely asked a staff member named Jasmine  if she could help slice the bread.

Unfortunately, she responded with a blunt "No" in a rather rude manner. When questioned further she explained that she could not do it because the staff member responsible for the machine had already gone home. Given that the machine could have sliced the loaf in about a minute, the response felt unhelpful and dismissive.

Thankfully, the cashier Hwee Tin was very kind and went out of her way to assist us by slicing the bread herself. Her willingness to help turned an otherwise disappointing experience into a positive one.

I appreciate the cashier's excellent customer service, but I hope the store can ensure that staff like Jasmine are more courteous and willing to assist customers where possible.

This CS Fresh branch has ‘Craft Beer on Tap’ although Hoegaarden can hardly be considered as craft beer. Little Creatures was good though, at $7 for half pint. You need to pay at the normal queue before you can collect your beer. Would be great if the beers are served in glasses instead of plastic cups.

Medium-ish cold storage but the only supermarket near the Buona Vista MRT outside of Holland Village area. Has a small bread and cheese, roast meats and salads, the usual. Three checkout counters. Generally fine but not as extensive a selection as the larger ones.

Looks modern but there are a lot of cages with carton boxes stacked near the checkout making it look ugly. It is rather small and the range are limited: eg I can’t seem to find Ben And Jerry Vegan ice-cream here, can’t of out of touch for a top end supermarket. Lots of stuff out of stock or do not stock: yalkult grapes, Meiji chocolate milk, etc. But other than this, the ambience is great. The checkout lady can improve on her customer service skills too just like the other Cold Storages: position premium but do not bother to train their checkout girls.

Would like to confer my sincere thanks and appreciation to Mui Ling for helping me to recover my laptop. I had left my laptop in the store which is completely my fault. The laptop contains all the important data. Mui Ling kept it safe for two days and returned it to me as I had left it today. Had it not for her help, both the laptop and the data would have been lost, which would be a disaster. My thanks to Mui Ling and the Cold Storage team at Star Vista for your professionalism, integrity, honesty and top-notch customer service!
My friendly neighborhood store - they also provide parcel and packages collection service, they even know their customers by name. Always with a smile and reliable services.

Always cheerful staff. I have my Lazada deliveries come here. And if I ever need anything for the house I come here to support the mom and pop shop rather than go to the NTUC Fairprice. Happy to see small businesses like this around.
very cheerful staff and super efficient in parcel sorting!!!

A quintessential minimart and definitely an integral icon of the dover court estate. Run by friendly owner and staff. Really appreciate the wide array of household items, drinks, snacks and quick dry cleaning services. There's nothing you can't find at Jumbo Minimart!

Very friendly staff and very well stocked convenience store.

i am so pleasantly surprised at the range of items they stock.

They have  everything from stationery ,toiletries, food items, ice cream , toys etc.

They also do print outs and laundry.

Very convenient for all residents of Dover community.

I would like to raise a concern regarding one of your cashiers at NUHS Medical Building at 6pm. On multiple occasions, I have observed that she behaves rudely toward customers, which creates an unpleasant experience.
Additionally, she frequently watches videos on her phone at the counter with the volume on, which is both unprofessional and disruptive. This affects the overall service quality and environment.
I hope this issue can be addressed promptly to maintain a respectful and professional standard of service.
Indian old lady cashier staff extremely rude. Very unwilling and unhappy 🙁 to serve. Please, remove her for the benefit of all value customers.
Not much to say about Cheers itself, but the staff here are some of the sweetest people I’ve ever met. They’re always greeting customers with a smile, regardless of the hour I visit.
Cashier was not attentive and give wrong information when doing redemption, 2/10 for customer service attitude hostile
Cashier doesn’t know Yuu points

Since childhood, this place has been standing tall for over 30 years.

Question is, why is it unlike other markets in the west? Housewives will probably tell you that there are more variety of fishes and “sea creatures” sold here compared to everywhere else. 😄

It is true, to some extend because I was still able to find Spanish Mackerel or also known as Tenggiri / Batang , at 1pm ! If it was Bukit Panjang Market, you will never find it by 10-11 am since it is quite a popular type of fish.
