Wanted to look for the elusive Toli Shad also known as Terubuk but did not manage to since I was in a hurry.

Nevertheless, Gombak Market is like a “vintage watch haven”. Sorry if the comparison is way put of base here. 😂

Come on down and see for yourself what this interesting Wet Market has to offer. Not forgetting the wide selection of breakfast available at its many coffeeshops under one roof.

I went this time on a Saturday. Normally weekends very crowded. Good place to come, everything found.  Can have breakfast, McDonald, roti prata, and Chinese food and dim-sum. Besides Giant and Prime supermarkets, there is also a small wet market, whereby fish, meat, vegetables and fruits are found here.

Often bring my mother in law here to get her groceries. Exceedingly crowded during the weekends and with the social distancing procedures in placw, there really isnt much place for the crowd to practise social distancing. Stallholders are having a difficult time controlling the crowds while selling their products. Would be unfair for them if the crowd refuse to listen to them.

Would be awesome if HDB could build a 2 storey wet market like some other matured estates such as toa payoh to ease the crowds as well as space the stores further from each other.
Bukit gombak wet market like mini gelang serai everything you need can get there the price not so expensive and many shop surround there.morning time very crowded.
Love going there..2 stn away frm my plce (yewtee) do marketing n have brunch 😍

I came to this gym as a beginner, and it’s been a great experience so far. The environment is welcoming, and the equipment is clean and easy to use. They even have a rowing machine. It’s a really good place to start if you’re new to working out.
Vibrant good energy in this gym, makes me motivated to work out and keep fit! ☺️ so glad there’s such a welcoming gym in this area~

Absolutely thrilled to have this new fitness centre in the area! The facility is modern, spotless, and equipped with everything needed for a great workout.

What really sets it apart though, is the recovery experience which I'm totally glad I'd been talked into trying — the infrared sauna is incredibly relaxing and helps ease muscle soreness after a great workout, while the 5°C cold plunge although numbing at first, leaves you feeling energized and revitalized.

Most importantly, the staff are welcoming, knowledgeable, and genuinely passionate about helping members achieve their fitness goals. For someone who only ever does cardio in the gym, I had plenty of questions regarding the other equipment, but the staff patiently answered every single one of them.

The atmosphere here is motivating without being intimidating, making it a great place for both beginners and experienced gym-goers.
💯💯💯 recommend!!

This gym is great for both regular gym-goers and complete beginners. As someone who rarely goes to the gym, I’d highly recommend checking out Homeground Balestier if you’re looking for a welcoming place to start or continue your fitness journey.

The team is experienced, approachable and incredibly welcoming. On my first visit, they took the time to answer my questions and made me feel comfortable right away, which can make all the difference for someone new to the gym environment.

The space is modern, well-maintained and thoughtfully equipped, with a good range of machines, free weights and training equipment to support different workout styles and fitness levels.

My favourite feature is the recovery zone, which includes both a sauna and cold plunge. Combined with the clean shower facilities and thoughtful touches like Dyson hair dryers, it makes for a really refreshing way to end a workout.

Overall, a well-designed gym with a friendly atmosphere and excellent facilities :) Please check them out!

Strategically located in Balestier, this new gym ticks all the right boxes for me as a regular gym-goer. It also has a sauna and cold plunge facility which you won’t normally find in a gym. Would absolutely recommend!

Awesome and supportive environment! Coaches know us by name and it feels like one big family. It is also very convenient and just a few minutes walk from Kent Ridge MRT! 6am class is my perfect way to start the day ✨💪🏽🤩 A very friendly community and I love the structured and progressive training programme. Thank you BFT Kent Ridge!! 😍

Good environment equipped with amenities! The trainers are experienced and friendly to guide us during the training. They even conducted extra fundamental sessions on Sat afternoon to make sure we are doing the right posture. Will recommend more friends to join me Definitely!

Really lovely branch of BFT, the details of the gym are thought out very carefully which makes a difference during workouts. The owners and coaches are all very friendly and helpful; they make the extra effort to ensure that the sessions are conducted in a safe and conducive manner for clients of all skill levels.

This branch has also proactively engaged other business owners in the area for client perks (read, DISCOUNTS) ☕️🍱🍛🍕wooot!

This BFT is AMAZING! The coaches here are truly like the most supportive crew ever. It's almost like a community.  Everyone helps and supports each other to be BIGGER BETTER and STRONGER. Also, they use fancy SOAPS and CONDITIONERS from OMNO. I always smell like a goddess after class. Wah. Shiok.

one of the most pleasant BFTs i’ve been to. super motivating coaches that will guide and push you for all classes. Clean and accessible via all modes of transport. Fantastic playlists for classes as well 😬 10/10 recommend

Pitch is new and a few more months to run in as there are patches of irregular surface (non flat). Parking is good as Orto is just next to it with many F&B options as well. Other feedback from fellow Futsal mates is that the pitch is long with narrow width hence need a bit of getting used to by changing certain strategic formation. Overall, good pitch that will certainly be better as time gets by

Great environment to enjoy a game of football.

Would have been better if there are water coolers on site.
Nice place for soccer games. Equipped with vending machines for drinks and a small snack bar.

Chef sharif let me try the pizza coming soon on the menu. Very good amount of cheese. And not too heavy. Would like it more if the dough had a bit more burn.

Looking forward to the menu!
To find free attractions in Singapore nearly impossible. This is place is one of the free one. And very beatifull. It is only one rain forest which is located in a city. Don't miss.

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

Very clean and movie quality very clear and good!👍🏻✨
Cinema here is new so come check it out while it's still very new can clean🤣🤣
Overall very nice, staffs also very friendly. Bought tickets through machine easy to use.
👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨✨👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨

my first time at shaw theatres jem! very clean and the seats were really comfortable
i was surprised by the sound system here, it’s really really good, even for a normal digital screening! out of the three times i’ve watch project hail mary this was by far the best experience
5 stars 🤩

first time coming to jem for a premium movie and it was a good experience. we only got fries but i was surprised they had ice creams and alcohol available too. the whole atmosphere is calm with dim lighting, plush seats and tons of legroom. it’s super family friendly but still feels grown up so you can actually relax. kamalii gave us a quick tour and explained all about the premium options so we knew what we were getting. the premium waiting area looks excellent and the service was really good, way better than the other outlets i’ve been to. she made us feel like vips, not just another booking. we’re definitely coming back here soon.

Such a little jewel of a cinema in Singapore. It has a wonderfully quaint, retro charm that makes the whole experience feel special and nostalgic in the best way. In a time when so many cinemas feel generic, this place has real character and soul. The atmosphere is intimate, cosy, and full of old-school charm, which makes watching a film here feel far more memorable. Definitely the kind of hidden gem that deserves more love and support. A must-visit for anyone who appreciates unique independent cinemas. We really gotta support places like this!
Lovely cinema! Really love the retro aesthetic of the lobby. Glad that Filmhouse is carrying on The Projector's legacy of providing a space for alternative cinema!

I am glad that there's a place like Filmhouse in Singapore to appreciate and support quality movies for the movie-fans. The selection of movies that Filmhouse is choosing to screen seem to be artistic and award-winning ones - both from the past and newly released. This is definitely a place for movie fans to come from time to time. The seats are pretty small and uncomfortable, but good for the old-school vibes. Their toilets are really cool with many movie posters too.

Just one thing; if you are tall (like above 175cm), please do not take first half rows. You will be blocking the subtitles. Be considerate and sit at the back.
Great place, chill vibes, and friendly people. A bunch of cultured cinema goers’ heaven. Classics being screened every so often, definitely recommend.
Good movie theatre with nostalgic taste! And their popcorn is very nice!

There were a bunch of rude old seniors who attempt to do taiji quan every Friday night. They refused to move and were hogging the basketball court when they could have done their activities at the empty badminton court next door. Despite me and my friend's attempts to negotiate with them and request for them to share the space, they possessed an entitled mentality and told us we had to move as they "were going to take out the weapons soon". They also discriminated against my dyslexic friend. When we tried to discuss the issue with them, they became rude and threatening, saying they would upload pictures of us. They did take multiple photos of us against our consent and showcased xenophobia by stating we were foreigners. To top it off, they then questioned our Singaporean identity. These senile individuals should not be allowed to occupy the CC and I request for the management to curtail their activities and tell the organisers to manage the behaviour of their members. On top of this, we had a valid booking for the court which they disregarded. What a waste of a lovely basketball facility

I visit this CC only once every 4 yrs. Since my son was toddler until now, he goes by himself now.

Good memories....
Once again, we r back to the World Cup, Qatar.

Thanks to the CC n members for organising this, esp for those who can't afford the hefty cable TV costs.

Special shout out also to the helpers who were friendly and patient to us football fanatics. 👍👍👍

Offer very useful and fun tuinl service; there is a post office, Pops parcel receiving and sending, community club services like dance classes, badminton hall and basketball courts. There is also a electronic recycle dropoff box here which use regularly. The building looks a bit dated but it still serves the residents living here well.

Bukit Timah Community Club is located at 20 Toh Yi Drive. Offering a host of activities and facilities for your use at an affordable price or courses available for all age groups. With additional school holiday program for the young to spend some healthy, creative with play and learn science.
Went for my Covid vaccination. It was well organized. I arrived at 8.20pm and was out at 9.30pm.
One of the most fun playground concepts that allow kids who love to climb to have a whale of a time. The slides are high and fast so they make the payoff worth it. Relatively shaded!

I prefer to walk here instead of Mt Faber becos this hill is less touristy. And hence less crowded.  Weekdays are even more peaceful & tranquil.  Air is fresh… Gd for de-stress.
However u can still walk to Mt Faber then to
Vivo City as they are all linked under the southern ridges.
Pls note currently the path fm Telok Blangah Hill to Hort Park is closed/ blocked due to upgrading works.

From Commonwealth, we jogged and walked all the way to Telok Blangah Hill Park. The climb was challenging, with steep stairs uphill, but the experience was absolutely worth it! 🏃‍♂️⛰️ The views and sense of accomplishment at the top made every step feel rewarding. A great spot for fitness enthusiasts who love a bit of a challenge. 💪🌿

Visit the hill park during weekday evening.

Take grab and alight at car park, very easy to spot the the hill.

Attach photo do show that is not many people around during weekday.

Top of the hill really windy, nice place to relax to chit chat with your close one.

Disappointed that there is no "bed of flower" during the visit, maybe not the season or something else.

28 Sep 2023: Oops! The entrance to Forest Walk is temporarily closed...expected completion is 30 Nov 2023.
12 Jul 2022: Cool breeze, scenic elevated walkway to Alexandra Arch and not forgetting the amazing “birds orchestra”
08 Jun 2022: Turn left or turn right? One will bring you to the elevated walkway of Forest Walk and Hort Park and the other will bring you to the iconic Henderson Waves and Mount Faber Park. Nice quiet environment...take a moment to listen to the birds singing or watch the squirrels in their natural surroundings.

Good place for a short hike where you can enjoy the greenery away from the hustle and bustle of the city without the need to travel too far. A secondary forest still rich in biodiversity where you can spot birds on the treetops and beautiful flowers hidden within the greenery.

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

If you love nature, I recommend you doing a hike here. It’s good for beginners and all the trails are colour coded for the difficulty level. Along the trails, there will be sheltered post for you to rest. You can easily spot some common dwellers such as snakes, monkeys and wild boar. If you are lucky enough, you may find some cool birds. I come here almost every week and spend 1-2hrs doing the hike.

A lovely small rainforest park tucked away in the west that feels surprisingly immersive despite its compact size. The walking trails are well-paved and shaded by tall trees, making it comfortable for both casual strolls and light workouts. There’s also a playground and a simple fitness corner, so it works well for families as well as those looking for a quick exercise session

One of the highlights is the lookout point overlooking the quarry — a peaceful spot where you can pause, enjoy the greenery, and listen to the sounds of nature. Wildlife sightings are quite common too; it’s not unusual to spot colourful birds, squirrels, and even monkeys if you’re lucky. History buffs will appreciate the remnants of WWII structures such as old pillboxes scattered around the park, which add a subtle layer of heritage to the natural scenery

The only downside at the moment is that the path leading to the Syonan Chureito (the WWII Japanese remembrance site) is under maintenance, so access is temporarily closed

It was my first and hope it's not the last visit to this place. I really want to go there again.

Really liked the place and enjoyed walking around this place. This would definitely be a cute for all of your sadness.

Didn't realise there was a quarry in the park, took a long walk in nature and found many wonderful things! Was caught by a sudden downpour, appreciated the thick trees around the area which sheltered me from the rain.

Good mix of trails, some paths are cobblestones so be in a proper footwear.

Caught a squirrel in action too!

🌳A lush greenery that is just a stone throw away from the Bukit Batok Housing Estate, providing residents living here a green lung to relax, exercise and be with nature.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️Many came here in the morning for exercises and brisk walk.
🛝🏋️At the Bt Batok Nature Park, besides having numerous shelters and walking paths, there is a Children Playground for little ones to enjoy, a Foot Reflexology Path and an Adult Fitness Corner that are next to the Playground.
🏞️But the centrepiece is the Lookout Point, where one can rest, take in the sight of the tranquil water and it's scenic surrounding.
⛩️Another interesting place around the Park with World War II history is Syonan Chureito Memorial
🚧Unfortunately, the access path from the park proper is closed for safety reason and is undergoing stabilisation work which is expected to be completed end Dec 2027.
Nevertheless, the site can be reached from Lorong Sesuai.
🪨Fun fact:
Bukit Batok Nature Park was formerly a granite quarry site.
Like many other granite quarry around, it ceased operation for safety and environmental reasons. And was then transformed into a nature park for all to enjoy.
Bukit batok natural park nice and peaceful park see sunrise depend on the cloud good place exercise 2 jan 2026

To be fair, I didn’t have the time to explore The Learning Forest as much as I wanted to. I'm sure there are many more things to see.

I enter the are through Ginger Garden and passed by Tyersall Gate Visitor Center. This would be the best point to get to if you want to go to the Learning Forest without walking the whole botanical garden. I saw travel bus as well besides private cars and taxi/car-sharing.

Upon entering from here, I was greeted by a nice Orchid Lake, which is wonderful.

I believe they have guided tours as well if one is interested.

This part of Botanical garden is more quiet and relaxing. Great to take small walk with kids to the giant tree top and around the swan lake (although only 1 swan to see). Convenient parking at learning forest carpark and lots of space  even on weekends
I explored the Learning Forest at the Botanical Gardens and enjoyed discovering its diverse ecosystems and native wildlife in a peaceful setting.

Beautiful place to visit if you are in Singapore or if you live in Singapore. The enclosed area is temperature controlled so it's cool inside. Lots of beautiful flowers and plants to learn more about.

This is a nice place to start your touring of the Botanic Gardens. You get to learn something about the trees and plants in Singapore. Watch out for the chickens too. You can see them every now and then. Quite a sight.
beautiful flats by HDB. also many thank to our migrant workers for building homes for us.
Beautiful Pigeon hole architecture. Makes me feel like a bird.
My house is here
228B Central Drive

A beautiful, relaxing and vibrant place for walks, for taking a step back from the hectic pace.
Spending 3 hours walking and appreciating the fresh air, the manicured gardens, the lake and wonderful architecture, helped to destress and reset.
If you've not been, well worth some time out.

Definitely one of the nicest and must go to parks in Singapore if you are alone, couples, families group of friends wanting to escape from the concrete jungle of the city. Jurong used to be perceived as a very faraway venue to commute to but with so many new MRT train lines it’s so much easier and faster to reach. Do take note of opening hours. Free entry and easily accessible with many entrances by Chinese Garden MRT or Lakeside MRT and buses. Great for all activities, walking , strolling, jogging, cycling, picnics and there is even a skate park and dog run. What makes this park stand out from the rest is the sheer size, the large lake with canoeing; stunning replicas of historical Chinese and Japanese architecture and pagodas, bonsai garden and wildlife. And especially the stunning Lily Flower Pond at the Japanese Garden and the Lotus Flower Pond at the Chinese Garden. Definitely worth to make a trip down to enjoy this green oasis escapade rich in flora and fauna and water. It’s very tranquil and peaceful from Mondays to Saturdays. Ample toilets and drinking water dispenser and also a beverage shop selling nice cold beverages.

NParks really did a good job in revamping this park.
There's plenty of interesting plants and wildlife to spot.
Took me and my friends 3 hours to casually explore the entire area.
It's definitely a nice place to run in the mornings/evenings.

I highly recommend to visit this gardens, as there are a lot of attractions to see and they are all free! It’s a beautiful area to dive into culture and nature, just 40 min from Singapore with the bus. Very relaxing and a moment to take a pause from the overtourism and consumistic view of marina bay and the Singapore center.
Not as big as the botanic garden, but I’d say it’s better to view this than that.
Really nice gardens. Will be even more pleasant to walk in when the trees in the still new Japanese and Chinese gardens grow older and provide more shade. Looking forward to that!

While my wife had her off days, we decided to head out for a jog along the Ulu Pandan Park Connector Dover. Starting from our place in Commonwealth, we made our way to this peaceful and scenic trail and it did not disappoint.

I actually discovered this place earlier during one of my solo mountain bike rides. The path was so enjoyable that I told my wife we had to try jogging here together. And we finally did! The trail stretches all the way to Clementi, making it perfect for long jogs, casual walks, or a smooth cycling session.

Surrounded by greenery and tucked away from the busy roads, it’s truly a great spot to relax, stay active, and enjoy the outdoors. Definitely a place we’ll keep coming back to.

Ulu Pandan Park Connector is a well-loved green corridor in the West, and the Dover stretch — running from Clementi to Ghim Moh and linking to the Rail Corridor — is a pleasant, functional route for walking, running, or cycling. It’s well-paved, spacious, and lined with greenery, making it a reliable choice for daily exercise or weekend outings.

While it’s not as scenic or wildlife-rich as the Faber Hills/Heights section further west (a favourite for birdwatchers), this stretch offers solid connectivity and a strong community vibe. For a complete outing, I recommend parking at Ghim Moh, doing a loop along the connector, and then popping into Ghim Moh Market for a meal — there are plenty of great hawker stalls worth checking out.

I’d rate it a 4 out of 5 — accessible, peaceful, and a practical link between key parts of the city with a rewarding food stop at the end.
Beautiful connector connecting Ghim Moh to the former Jurong Railway Bridge in Sunset Way. It doesn't get overly crowded. A great place for a morning workout.

The Ulu Pandan Park Connector (Dover) is a part of Singapore’s network of park connectors, designed to provide green links for pedestrians and cyclists to explore the city’s natural landscapes. Located near the Dover area, this park connector connects several key locations, including Ulu Pandan Canal, offering a scenic and peaceful route for outdoor activities.

The park connector is surrounded by lush greenery and is home to various species of plants and wildlife, making it a popular spot for nature walks, cycling, and birdwatching. It also provides access to nearby parks, including Dover Forest, where visitors can enjoy the tranquility of the natural surroundings.

The Ulu Pandan Park Connector is well-maintained, with smooth pathways and clear signage, ensuring that visitors can enjoy a safe and enjoyable experience while exploring Singapore’s natural beauty. It’s a great spot for those looking to escape the city’s hustle and bustle while staying active and immersed in nature.
One of the best place in touch with Nature.

This particular Park Connector rans along Sungei Pandan Kechil section of West Coast Road to West Coast Highway.
It's a short 450 metre paved path.
Despite its short length, it's heavily utilised.
Residents living around the estate would walk or jog up and down this short stretch of the Connector in the morning and, in the evening.
The path would be exposed to  full sun in the early morning hrs. And seniors just love walking or do stretching exercises in the sun.

A very pretty park connector that’s perfect for running and cycling, linking West Coast Park with Clementi Woods Park. I often see people jogging here, and with the new HDB and elderly home completed, it’s even more convenient. It connects residents to nearby bus stops, the community centre, and other places easily.

Overall, Millennia Institute serves as a valuable alternative pathway to the A-Levels. It is particularly suitable for students who prefer a steadier academic pace, appreciate a supportive environment, and are willing to take ownership of their learning over a longer period.

horrible school, horrible bio.dept, little to NO support to students due to lack of funds 🤑, they’re falling behind other jc.

the exam papers are so easy that they provide a sense of security for students, not preparing them for the actual examination ahead.

Worst place ever. So many Hdb are near it and so many events are happening there like sports day which they always need to shout plus Every Monday or Friday night a very big light will be flashed at the Hdb at 446A and 445A literally can u stop using a mic I could hear it on the forth floor and it made my baby cry. Like why the hell do you need to make the residents nearby mad?

As an alumni, this is a piece of advice I can give you. Only join if you think that you have the discipline and determination in order to excel in your A-levels. Over the course of three years, there is a possibility of getting distracted due to the relatively laid-back environment. Not to mention, you may also come across negative influences from your peers. While the school does provide the necessary resources to help you pass your exams, it ultimately depends on you to decide if you are willing to put in the extra effort and strive for excellence.

Was invited to give the valedictorian speech during 2007 institute day but another name ended up on the roll of honour. If you wanted someone to fulfil just that segment of the event, you could just record that ‘outstanding student’s speech and play it during the event. Shouldn’t have wasted my time.
Doing additional duties like Tentage and MRT with limited manpower.
Lost my way.
Singapore Polytechnic Gate 1 will lead to Open Carpark A.
Great gate

place_name: Cambridge Learning Group SG – Jurong West Tuition Centre for Primary & Secondary English, Maths & Science Exam Specialists
review_text:
I am very happy by the amazing progress my child has made in Maths on his secondary school year! He went from just passing on S1 to topping the class on S2 after attending Mr Koh class.
Much appreciated the support and guidance we received.
Highly recommend for Mr Koh’s dedication. I am very grateful for the hard work and encouragement from Cambridge Learning Group.

place_name: Cambridge Learning Group SG – Jurong West Tuition Centre for Primary & Secondary English, Maths & Science Exam Specialists
review_text: Cambridge Learning Group SG has been wonderful for my son, Ethan. He has improved tremendously in his P3 Science and recently achieved AL1, the highest possible grade. We are very thankful to the teachers for their clear explanations, structured lessons, and patient guidance. Ethan enjoys the classes and has become much more confident in his learning. Highly recommended for any parent looking for quality and effective tuition support!

place_name: Cambridge Learning Group SG – Jurong West Tuition Centre for Primary & Secondary English, Maths & Science Exam Specialists
review_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

place_name: Cambridge Learning Group SG – Jurong West Tuition Centre for Primary & Secondary English, Maths & Science Exam Specialists
review_text: really benefitted me after i joined this tuition centre. i used to struggle in e-maths alot and did terribly for my prelims. right after prelims, i joined this tuition centre i could see more improvements in my work. i improved a lot for my o level emaths. im glad that i came here

place_name: Cambridge Learning Group SG – Jurong West Tuition Centre for Primary & Secondary English, Maths & Science Exam Specialists
review_text: Mr. Koh, our math tutor, has been exceptional in providing stable and reliable support throughout Hongkai’s studies. His guidance has been a great asset. I would strongly encourage any student looking to excel in math to join the Cambridge learning program.
You can use Chinese Garden MRT to reach here. This place is so relaxing and very comfartable to go walking, jogging and even just sitting at the bench.
During the week the gardens are very quiet.  The weekends it becomes much busier.  Weekends are great for extroverted folks week days are great for introverts.
Chinese Garden was beautiful.  A lovely location for a picnic & evening stroll
It’s good place to take snap photo.
Just across the japanese garden. Everything in this place is amazing! You can also enter the Live Turtle Tortoise museum. Oh, and the roaming lizard. :-)

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text: best old style hawker centre. only difference is, over here all the stalls are opened for business and doing great business. very lively and makes me hungry. all the food looks so good and delicious.

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text:
Update: 18 Mar 26

Today dabao-ed Chicken Pho (鸡肉荡粉), at $4.20 from Vietnam Pho SG for my daughter's lunch. She feedback that the meal is delicious 😋
___________________
I recently ordered a small plate of the fried black carrot cake after having previously tried the white version at 晶晶.

I'm pleased to say that both colors delivered on taste, they are equally good and absolutely worth the experience.
________________________
I believe this is one of the most affordable cai fan stalls in the western Singapore, yet they absolutely do not compromise on taste.

​For an incredible $3.50, I was able to get four generous items: half a salted egg, tender potatoes, fried onion egg, and a meatball. The quality and portion size for this price point are outstanding.

​Compared to the other two cai fan stalls in the hawker centre, 南山杂菜饭 is superior in both taste and pricing.

​价格亲民! 👍🏻👍🏻

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text:
More new big fans are installed
Food remains intact, as usual
My fav orders are
1. Chicken rice
2. Lor mee
3. Laksa
4. Teochew kueh
5. Penang char kway teow
6. Not a fan of mala, but here the mala is amazing.

Don’t forget to order the 金桔蜜 to hydrate your throat!

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text: Many food choices in the morning and lunch hours, however most stalls are closed for the day by 3pm.

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text: The best pork ribs soup is found in Yuhua market. This place is good for all meals after exercise at Chinese garden, opposite of the market.

This relatively new foodcourt offers great variety including few halal stalls. It's comfortable to dine there as the foodcourt is well ventilated and spacious. The highlights for me are the Vietnamese pho & banh mi, Prawn hokkien mee, Taiwanese braised pork rice, and local braised duck kueh teow/ rice.

While finally Bukit Batok got it's hawker centre, this is essentially a Food Court without air-conditioning. The number of food stalls(20+) pales compare to traditional hawkers that even only one available drink stall seems enough.

With all said, the food centre is airy with its high ceiling and non-congested table arrangements. They are good food amid its limited selections with Lor Mee my favorite.

I went to the fishball noodle and fish soup stall. Based on the menu the fish soup costs $6 for fish soup/fish porridge/fish beehoon. After I ordered the staff told me $13. So I asked how come additional charge. She said if normal $6 the fish very little only and said usually got noodle inside one so little. So I feedback should inform customers about it. They were not happy and face changed when I said I want $6 amount. Then they kept emphasising fish very little. I was like fine okay I just paid 50 cents more. Look at the pic how the sliced fish soup is like. I am just not so happy with how they just add $0.50 with the assumption of customers and themselves find it little really surprised me.

I will never patronised again.

Nice to eat were the duck rice stall, fishball noodle stall, wonton mee stall.

Not so nice were the chicken rice stall that had not fresh chicken, and newly opened japanese korean stall which we ate bibimbap and cutlet chicken with salmon with watery miso.

Totally love that there are huge fans that help provide some cooling in the hot weather! Ventilation is good. There are basins for washing of hands. Nearby playgrounds good to entertain the kids while we eat. The drinks were bad though, teh O kosong was so diluted tasted like water. Fried hokkien mee was too sweet!

Beef slices for vietnamese pho were overcooked so they were quite tough to chew on. The broth was fine. I think the best was the chicken rice set. Rice was fragrant and chicken breast wasn't too dry. Chili was very spicy, I wish they have spring onion ginger sauce too. Wanton mee, bak chor mee, sheng jiao bao and chicken wings were very good. Overall, delicious and affordable!

PACIFIC VEG RICE:
Mixed veg stall is quite pricey. Ordered 2 vegetables and 1 meat, and it cost MORE THAN $5, which feels a bit steep for cai fan. Given that the meat was mostly covered in thick flour. After checking the reviews, many others mentioned the same thing about the pricing. Unfortunately, I didn’t check beforehand. 😵‍💫 it did not match the price listed beside the cashier. When questioned, he mentioned that the sweet and sour pork alone cost $3.50.

Perhaps that also explains why there wasn’t much of a lunch crowd when I visited. Hopefully the pricing can be reviewed to make it more reasonable.

IROSHO JAP:
Closed down as of 31 March 2026. Loved their $11.50 teriyaki salmon rice.

The mixed rice stall started well with the lady serving. Recently, there’s this new uncle. The portioning provided by the uncle is utterly disgusting. The portion is super little and the price is outrageously high.

I hope the stall go review themselves on the staff they hiring. Please be fair.

Ordered the 御品fishball noodles plus noodles and vegetables. Total $5.80 but the lady serving gave all the vegetables which had turn black. Told her to change it but she only changed the first two pieces and put the bowl back on the tray. I used a chopstick and turn the vegetables over and realized below vegetables were even worst.  Showing a long black face she takes some vegetables from the fridge and change it. Add noodles portions also small.

Scammer !

2 meat 1 egg $7,50!

I ordered 2 meat dishes and 1 egg, and was charged $7.50, which is completely unreasonable for mixed rice. Prices were clearly displayed, and the amount charged did not match what was taken.

Very disappointing experience. I won’t be coming back, and I recommend others check prices carefully before ordering.

The whole coffee shop was warm without much ventilation. The sushi wasn't made fresh, they were pre-made for orders. Prawn noodles wasn't worth it at all, expensive $6.90 for the measly amount of ingredients, was given pork ribs but mostly bones without much meat. Only ONE prawn sliced into two halves. The broth was also salty. Nah, never again will I come to this coffee shop.

Superb atmosphere. Food is nice especially for the price point! Very friendly staff too. And not to mention the food always comes fast regardless if the place is crowded or not. Really enjoy having my meals here!
The food is great, especially considering how affordable it is overall! Staff are friendly too! This is a very convenient and much needed addition to the neighbourhood! There's even outdoor seating.
Opening day visit - great food and service.
Beautiful restaurant and food standards are always so consistent! Shoutout to Julia for always giving the best service on every visit ❤️
Clean spacious and kids friendly! Food was served fast and staffs were friendly! Will be back again’

I can’t believe it! I just ordered from a home-based business and unboxed a whole bunch of surprises ☕💕

Ordering was super easy, even simpler than big coffee chain app. Everything went smoothly and my drinks were ready faster than I expected.

When I stepped out of the lift, the cute bright logo instantly caught my eye and led me straight to the right place. At the doorstep, a coffee cup and coffee bean plushie greeted me and that already made my day 🥰 My drinks made it home perfectly with zero spillage.

The moment I collected the coffee, I could already smell the amazing aroma ☕ It reminded me of the scent from Flash Coffee, something I haven’t been able to find in big coffee chains. And the sweetest part, the owner even added lovely greeting and handwritten note on the cup! That little touch really warmed my heart ❤️

Now I’ve found that special coffee vibe again with CuppaCoffee. I truly enjoyed my drink so much. Please keep Coconut Latte( October special) in your menu, it’s my new must-have 🥥 October please don’t end yet because I need more Coconut Latte in my life! 💛

Tried the coconut latte and it was sooo good! Best coconut latte ever ever! So rich, creamy, fragrant and not too sweet. My husband who dislikes coffee loved it too!!! Definitely try this delicious treat if you're ever in Tengah!

had the dirty earl drink and it was so yummy! the drink is layered with earl grey infused cream, milk and espresso which blends well together. i recommend this drink especially if you are an earl grey lover!!! its also not too sweet and the coffee goes down smoothly (not bitter or sour). the owners were also very friendly! would order again!
Such a great cup of coffee! You can really taste the quality beans and care that goes into each brew. Smooth, flavourful, and made with love – definitely worth coming back for.
Tried their matcha strawberry indeed the nicest I had so far from all those home based cafe 🍵😍 not even exaggerating! Try it u will know! Their coffees are great as well!

While I did not encounter any rude staff per se, and in fact one of the staff at the payment counters was actually pretty friendly, the odd thing was that nearly every aisle had someone doing stocktaking. And some of them were more concerned with their stocktaking then allowing customers to view the products. This was especially for the staff with the ladder, he saw me trying to look at the products where he was, but carried on nonchantly even though I was trying to move round different angles to see the products there. Wonder why they could not do it any other time, but even if they had to perform the stocktaking, allow customers opportunity to see the items, odd that customers are not important to them. And seeing the other reviews probably reinforces the mentality at this outlet. I have more photos, but guess will still try to observe not taking any face front.

On 28th November at approximately 11:00am. The behaviour displayed by one of your staff members was concerning, highly unprofessional, and left both myself and other customers uncomfortable.

While I was near the entrance of the store, a staff member suddenly began shouting from within the supermarket and continued loudly all the way to the front where the trolleys are stationed. Her raised voice immediately drew the attention of many shoppers. Upon reaching the entrance, she proceeded to harshly reprimand a customer who was simply taking a trolley from the trolley station—an area clearly intended for customers to collect trolleys.

The staff member loudly asked, “Why are you taking the trolley from there? There are trolleys in the back at the corner. You people like to take trolleys from there and anyhow park.”
Her tone was rude and accusatory, and her remarks were unwarranted. It was both puzzling and unreasonable, as the trolley station is logically and visibly the place where customers would collect trolleys. There were no signs indicating that trolleys should instead be taken from the back, nor would an average customer know to do so. Furthermore, the claim that customers would “anyhow park” the trolley seems illogical, considering coins must be returned upon locking the trolley back, leaving little room for misuse.

I believe this situation could have been handled calmly, politely, and with basic customer service etiquette.

A convenient supermarket at Plantation Plaza with a good selection of daily essentials, groceries, and fresh produce. The store is clean, well-organized, and the staff are polite and helpful. Great place for quick and easy shopping.

I saw this chinese lady many times working at the self service kiosk. At first i thought she had a bad day, but if many times even today i ask for plastic , her body language was so rude no eye contact , eyes staring at us but from a side glance and face is so grumpy like we owe her money.  Give plastic but body and face facing other side while showing arrogant annoyed face, she is so rude . other Giant staffs not smiling it’s okay , just a neutral face i am okay as i understand people are tired, they can still give plastic facing us politely .  This lady staff need to improve on her customer service. High management please check on her attitude towards customers. We Tengah residents need helpful staff not a rude one serving us.

The spacious interior arrangement is a definite plus, with wide walkways that make shopping a breeze, not a squeeze! The powerful air conditioning adds to the comfort, making every visit enjoyable regardless of the weather outside. Thanks, Giant, for bringing such convenience and comfort to our neighborhood—I couldn't ask for more from a local supermarket!

Anytime Fitness Tengah is a fantastic gym with all the amenities you need for a great workout experience! The gym is spacious, well-equipped, and spotlessly clean, with a wide variety of machines and free weights for every fitness level. Whether you’re into cardio, strength training, or functional exercises, they have it all.

The staff is friendly and professional, always ready to assist with any questions or provide guidance. There’s also 24/7 access, which is perfect for those with a flexible schedule. Plus, the atmosphere is welcoming and not intimidating, making it a great place for beginners as well as experienced gym-goers.

The facility also has clean changing rooms and showers, and there’s plenty of parking available nearby. Overall, a top-notch gym in a convenient location—definitely worth checking out!

Nice and clean gym with well-maintained equipment and a good variety for different workouts. The space feels comfortable and not too crowded.
One thing that could make the experience even better would be having a water dispenser inside the gym. Overall, a great place to work out in Tengah.

Only thing missing for me is a punching bag. Would be damn nice to have one for boxing or just to release stress after a long day 💀
Overall still a solid gym, definitely one of the better outlets I’ve been to.

Found a hidden gem! Discovered a convenient 7-11 right beside the famous Thai restaurant. It has ample space with side tables and chairs—perfect for a quick and comfortable dine-in. Best part? This store has the Slurpee machine—we absolutely loved it!

The tattooed Indian lady who is usually on the night shift, including today (31/3), has been frequently absent from the counter. She was not present at the shop for almost 15 minutes.

Kindly look into this matter and take the necessary action to ensure proper staffing at the counter.

Beware of this 7-11 prices. I went to buy Po Chai pills and label stated $7.50. The attitude cashier told me to use self check-out instead and price became $9.80. I went to the staff to verify and she scanned the item with price of $9.40. Double check the price when checking out.

The Indian lady staff has 0 integrity at all! I bought a wrap that has the 25% discount label, went to the counter to pay but she asked me to use the self service kiosk to pay instead despite that she isn’t doing anything at the counter and she’s looking at me using the kiosk. Yesterday was my baby first trial class and im impressed! The front desk are very friendly and helpful to share process.
Coach Ya Lee was also very engaging for my baby. The environment and facilities are very spacious, organised and clean.
Cant wait for our next session!
I attended a trial lesson, and it was excellent experience. My child is two years old, and the coach gave a patient one-to-one lesson. Will sign up 5 classes continue learning.

We chose the Tengah outlet based on my sister’s recommendation, mainly because of the indoor heated pool and convenient changing/shower facilities, perfect for our 6-month-old baby so she wouldn’t get cold moving from the pool to the wash area. It is also fully sheltered from the carpark to the swim school.

We came for a baby trial lesson with Coach Phoebe today and it was an absolute delight seeing baby enjoy herself so much! Coach Phoebe was very professional, and very clear and concise with all the steps and instructions.

The pool water pH and chlorine levels are kept at safe levels for babies, and the shower/changing area was very clean and well-maintained too.

A huge plus is that existing students can also book the pool for practice sessions when there are no swim classes. Overall, such a wonderful experience!
We went for the intro lesson - teacher Phoebe was very patient with our boy & he really enjoyed his time. Teacher Phoebe was also very clear w her instructions as well.

Our first experience at Happy Fish Tengah was a fantastic introduction to the water that felt both safe and encouraging. The facilities were impressively clean and the pool temperature was perfectly "baby-warm," which made the transition into the water much smoother for my little one. Our instructor was incredibly patient, using a gentle mix of songs and play-based movements like [mention a skill, e.g., kicking or floating] to build confidence without any pressure.

Stepping into the Omni-Theatre at Science Centre Singapore felt like entering a completely different world. This isn’t your usual cinema — the massive dome screen and immersive surround sound make you feel like you’re inside the movie, whether you’re flying through space or exploring nature. The visuals are incredibly sharp and engaging, and it’s a great mix of entertainment and learning for both kids and adults.
One big tip: visit on a weekday. That’s truly the best time to go. The crowd is much smaller, and it feels almost like you have the theatre to yourself, especially if you’re going with family. The experience is more relaxed, quieter, and way more enjoyable without the usual weekend rush.
Ticket prices are also very reasonable. Singaporeans and Permanent Residents pay around $8, while tourists pay about $14, which is excellent value considering how unique the experience is compared to a regular movie theatre.
What I also love is how convenient the location is. After the Omni-Theatre, you can easily spend more time nearby. KidsSTOP™ is perfect for younger children with hands-on learning and play, while Snow City is just next door if you want something fun and cooling. It’s easy to turn this into a full half-day or even full-day family outing.
Overall, the Omni-Theatre is a great place to slow down, learn something new, and enjoy quality time together. If you’re looking for something different to do in Singapore — especially on a weekday — this is definitely worth checking out.

Brought my kids here today. It was pleasant even though 20mins into the show, one fell asleep and the other one was looking forward to the “good part” as we were expecting a show about space. However it was more of a NASA showcase. Commented that she came to learn about space, not rockets 😅

Not all of the footage made use of the unique omni dome but this could be due to the film’s origin. Sound could be improved. We sat at the top near the exit. Because the back speakers were off, the show lacked impact. It was also warm as if the aircon was off.

Parking was full. Some cars had to park on the side road inside carpark. Understood from staff that this was unusual as it’s the last day of Robofest at Science Centre. Perhaps some traffic control is required for busy days such as today.

We’ll come again for other shows and hope that the management look into the above especially the show experience. Still think it’s a core memory opportunity for kids as it did for me when I visited the omni theatre back in my school days.

As part of SG60, there was a promotion to visit the science centre and the Omni theatre free.  It was a bit confusing though as although the ticket listed this two places as free, you would have to book separately for each movie slot.  I learned later that each admission ticket that day could book one movie.  However they opened up another queue for walk in admission.  And I believed anyone who used the walk in managed to go in.

I managed to watch all the films that day except for Stars Dreaming.  Animal Kingdom was the most interesting film with nice visuals and interesting facts.  Cities of the future was quite a boring one that covered a few cities around the world on the issues of environment sustainability.  Some audiences actually walked out of it.  Kitz the cat's Secret of the Space Station was the only film with a storyline and also made use of the Onimax effect to make you nauseous and dizzy in a few scenes.  It was an animation targeted at children although the theme can be quite dark.  It was still educational.  I believed it was a Korean production and some dialogues were quite awkward. Overall I would only recommend Animal Kingdom.

I found the dome screen rather dim though.  But all the pixels on the screen were working fine.  The images were not clear and the animal kingdom felt like a low Res film.  The theatre is digital 8k though according to the staff.

In terms of environment, you need to put up with talking children and eating.  Even the mother next to me kept using her hp and I need to tell her about it.  Later she started to provide narration to her child.  So be prepared to put up with the annoyances of these people.

There were a lot of stairs climbing up and down.  If you are mobility challenged you could seek help from the staff and gained access via a lift.

This theatre along with the science centre would be torn down for another new building.  Not sure would there be an Omni theatre in the future.

Long ago I watched other shows like Grand Canyon, Space walk and diving in the sea.  All were good documentaries.

Got vouchers and brought my kids (5, 10, 12 and 14yo) to watch "Ocean Paradise". The venue is very clean (no eating and drinking allowed), comfortable and has a magnificent dome screen. The movie length of 35min was perfect for my 5yo (not too long) but also not so short that the trip wasn't worth it for my 14yo. The images from this show were stunningly beautiful and the messaging was clear. Nothing scary whatsoever, which is great for my easily-scared kids, but still having a strong storyline to keep everyone engaged throughout. Volume of sounds were also not too loud (unlike movie theaters) but still clear. Do check out the highly rated Indian Food stall just outside the omnitheater, or adjourn to the Regale for some pasta (tts what we ate anyways) if spice is not an option. Bottom line: fantastic immersive movie experience for kids (and adults)!!

I think the omni is losing its touch. The documentary is very boring. People expect to feel " they are in the movie" and yet we only feel like it is looking at a documentary. A lot of people end up sleeping.

There are actually 2 playgrounds within the Parc Flora estate. One is this Rooster playground that offers lots of climbing, slides and swing! The other (you can spot it from here) is a humongous treehouse playground - this one test the climbing skills of the little one!

They're located right next to the Tengah bus interchange, thus toilets and washing up is easy too!

My son started at this school since June 2024 and I’m really happy with his progress in development. He seems to be really enjoying himself and when I ask if he likes his teachers he always says yes! :)

I also like that the school organises many events for the children and also for parents to participate in together with them if they would like to.

Their curriculum also seems well thought out and I find that my son does more independent and imaginary play at home too.

Thank you to the caring and observant teachers for taking such good care of him. Would definitely recommend parents to check out Kensington!

Our Children's House Montessori is a great school which follows the Montessori curriculum. My child is having a wonderful time here. The environment is clean and welcoming and it encourages exploration. She enjoys sharing her experiences with us and she has become more vocal after joining the school. The teachers are very patient and caring and my child talks about some of the teachers very fondly. Another good thing about the school is the curriculum. The hands on learning is something my daughter enjoys and I am gradually seeing some of the independence that you hear about in the Montessori system.  I am so glad we chose this school for her. Do look up this school if you want your child to be independent and engaged in his/her learning while still having fun.

review_text:

place_name: Zenith Education Studio - Buona Vista | JC Tuition, Secondary Tuition & Pri | Math, Chem, Econs, Physics, GP, English
review_text:
I can honestly say that Zenith has been a game-changer throughout my JC journey. The level of support I’ve received here has truly made a difference in my academic life. Special thanks to my bio tutor, Melvin, and my chemistry tutor, Ding Jiang, for their unwavering dedication. They always go the extra mile to ensure I understand the material, and their willingness to take time out of their busy schedules for extra consultations has been incredibly helpful. What stands out the most is how approachable they are—no question ever feels too small or silly. They create a comfortable and open environment where I can ask anything without hesitation, and that has made a huge difference in my confidence and learning.
On top of the top-notch academic support, Zenith also knows how to keep students happy with amazing welfare! From acai bowls that refresh after a long day of lessons to delicious Japanese dons that make studying more enjoyable, the thoughtful little touches make all the difference. I’m really grateful for the experience and support I’ve had here—Zenith truly feels like a second home.

place_name: Zenith Education Studio - Buona Vista | JC Tuition, Secondary Tuition & Pri | Math, Chem, Econs, Physics, GP, English
review_text: I started lessons in Zenith at the start of J2 as my promo results were quite disappointing and I wanted to get some help before it was too late. My tutors Zach (H1 GP) and DJ (H2 Chem) were very friendly and approachable. They were always super patient whenever I had questions, helping me digest content in ways that are easily understandable. The lessons were well structured and I found the notes given very helpful, especially near exams! Overall, Zenith provided quite a comfortable learning environment so lessons didn’t feel that stressful and boring here

place_name: Zenith Education Studio - Buona Vista | JC Tuition, Secondary Tuition & Pri | Math, Chem, Econs, Physics, GP, English
review_text:
50th percentile to 100th percentile GP (ZACH BV)

gp felt like a new lingo. when I first entered jc, I knew that GP wasn’t a subject that I could “self-study” or mug mindlessly. My peers in jc boasted that GP was “just English” or “common knowledge” and yet, during assessments we would all be puzzled to have received an E/S, or an occasional D if we were super lucky. I knew that I had to take action as failing gp would directly affect my uni apps.hence, I stepped into Mr Zachary’s (Buona Vista) class with nothing up my arsenal, 0 skills and pure confusion.

IM PROUD TO SAY THAT IN LESS THAN A YEAR, Zach has transformed me from an average jc kid into topping my cohort for GP CA1(J2). I owe it all to Zach and I’m grateful to have him as my gp tutor and I wouldn’t trade him for the world.

Zach’s lessons were something I LOOKED FORWARD TO despite rigorous days in jc, not just because of how engaging they were( he’s lowkey kinda funny) , but because of the intention behind the way he teaches. He has a way of breaking down complex, often abstract ideas into something that feels intuitive and accessible, yet never oversimplified. Over the span of a year in which I was his student, I’ve come to realise that what he imparts goes far beyond mere GP content tested in the A lvls, he consistently pushes us to question, to reflect, and to engage more deeply within society and the world around us( he’s very woke).

What I appreciate most is how he treats our thinking as something worth developing seriously. HE DOES NOT EVER brush off our opinions or ideas, but helps us expand on them and phrase them in an intellectual way. He doesn’t just want us to do well academically and get A for GP, he expects us to grow into individuals who are socially aware, critical and hold ourselves accountable to our social responsibilities.

though he’s our teacher, he constantly makes the effort to engage with us as a friend. I can constantly see his efforts in making his classroom a safe and cohesive space, he’s incredibly easy to talk to and approachable. I’ve never ONCE hesitated on asking him questions or even making a joke. I could tell that Zach really does enjoy his interactions with his students, and more than anything, he’s always willing to help us out even at the VERY last minute. He’s always even me detailed feedback on my work, comments that are specifically tailored and well thought out to help me improve. crazy to me how much of a hustler he is , sending me pages worth of feedback enabling me to clutch promos.

stepping into his class was one of the BEST decisions I’ve made for myself, and I can sincerely say that j2 me is THANKING my j1 self for having him as my gp tutor. and yes, he’s the best tutor in zenith and there’s no pt comparing since he’s on a whole another lvl .

place_name: Zenith Education Studio - Buona Vista | JC Tuition, Secondary Tuition & Pri | Math, Chem, Econs, Physics, GP, English
review_text:
Duncan taught me Physics throughout my JC journey, and he’s genuinely one of the best teachers I’ve ever had. He explains concepts clearly and includes almost every type of question that could possibly appear, which really helped me feel prepared. He always goes the extra mile for us even when he doesn’t have to, which shows that he truly cares!

His classes are also always filled with laughter thanks to his humour and teaching style, which keeps lessons engaging and makes learning so much easier. I’m really grateful for all the help and support he’s given us! Highly recommend.

place_name: Zenith Education Studio - Buona Vista | JC Tuition, Secondary Tuition & Pri | Math, Chem, Econs, Physics, GP, English
review_text:
At the beginning of JC, GP felt like a subject that was almost impossible for me to tackle, which was really discouraging. I wasn’t very familiar with what the different components of the subject required, and the thought of memorising case studies for essays felt quite overwhelming. However, under Zach’s guidance, GP gradually became much less daunting. The techniques he taught us, often explained through simple acronyms that were easy to remember, helped me approach questions with more confidence. The weekly newsletters also helped me build up my example bank, which made it much easier to remember and apply examples in essays.

Joining Zenith is definitely something I don’t regret. I’m really grateful for Zach’s guidance, as well as the many enjoyable lessons (and welfare!) along the way. 🫶🏻

place_name: Stalford Learning Centre @ Tengah, Plantation Plaza
review_text:
Updated review:
Special thanks to Yvonne at Stanford Learning Center for going the extra mile to help with administrative matters. She was helpful, patient, and proactive — even taking the initiative to look into the issue and later updating me with a solution. I really appreciate her responsibility and thoughtful follow-up.

I had previously also been assisted by Jeevitha, who was very patient and thorough in explaining the courses and guiding me through the enrollment process. She made everything clear and stress-free, and I appreciated her dedication and helpful attitude.

It is good to see the branch maintaining a helpful and professional standard even after a staff change. Thank you to Yvonne, Jeevitha, and the team at Stalford Learning Center.

place_name: Stalford Learning Centre @ Tengah, Plantation Plaza
review_text:
I registered my daughter for K1 Chinese on 25 Sep. Later, we found out that she was the only child in the class. Since there were no other children for her to learn with, we decided to withdraw her in mid-Oct.

Stalford did not inform us that a withdrawal form was required. I waited until Nov to ask for an update on the deposit refund, only then was I told that the withdrawal form had to be submitted and that the refund would be issued by cheque within 3–5 weeks.

After waiting more than 5 weeks, I repeatedly followed up. An admin staff later called and mentioned that refunds by cheque would only be processed before Jun 2025, and that refunds are now done via PayNow instead. I was asked to give them a few days to check with their HQ.

Until now, there is still no update and the refund is still pending.

Please think twice before enrolling your children.

place_name: Stalford Learning Centre @ Tengah, Plantation Plaza
review_text: Stalford Learning Centre at Tengah Plantation offers an exceptional learning environment with dedicated teachers and a strong focus on student success. Highly recommend for anyone looking for quality education and a supportive learning atmosphere!
