place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.
Bus arrived timely, but for those who have to wait a long time this bus stop may not be very good due to its lack of seats. Thankfully it seems that this is only temporary
Home-based coffee! Tried their ice matcha oat milk and white. Balance blend of coffee and milk. Affordable price too! Will definitely order again!

Home-based coffee, came across this coffee from xiaohongshu. Remember to order via their Instagram page. I ordered ice white, the coffee is affordable and tastes good. I personally prefer less acidic coffee, and this suits my tastebuds.
First time ordering and was pleasantly surprised! The black sesame latte was fragrant and owner was thoughtful to prepare the drink fresh for collection. Will order other drinks to try next time!

Have been getting coffee from The Weekend Coffee almost every week as long as schedule allows! :) coffee is really fragrant and generous in portion! Owner will check for your timing of collection before preparing to ensure that the coffee is freshly made! Their sesame latte is super on point too, even my grandma loves it! Can’t wait to try their upcoming matcha!! :)

Great coffee! The amount of acidity is just right 😋 location is also convenient, just beside the CC

Their new menu item proffee is a must-try! Checks both your daily caffeine and protein requirement.

Visited Kopifellas Cafe at Plantation Plaza with two friends for lunch. We ordered their Set Meals, which come with a main and a drink—great value and very convenient.

​The Food:
​Minced Meat Noodles (Mee Pok): Two of us went for this. I opted to add extra ingredients to my order, and they were generous with the portions. The Mee Pok was cooked perfectly al dente—springy with a good bite. The seasoning was on point, coating the noodles well in a savory dark sauce without being overpowering.
​Laksa: My other friend ordered the Laksa with vermicelli. The broth was excellent—flavorful and "lemak" (rich/creamy) enough, hitting that perfect balance of spice and coconut milk.

​Verdict:
It’s a comfortable, modern spot to get your fix of heritage hawker favorites. We left satisfied and will definitely return to try their Toasts and Lor Mee next time.

Ordered ice  Kopi - C less sugar, but when it was served it looked darker than this photo I took.
Seems like it was ice Kopi less sugar without C

I asked and they confirmed 2 times it was ice Kopi C less sugar. It just doesn’t look like it. Either it was wrong or too little C added.

Since they confirmed and I tried it doesn’t taste like what it is, I ASKED them to add more C.

Paid $4.10 each for large cup.

Take pride in the work, people will compliment you without you even trying. Doesn’t it just ruin your day when you expect a good coffee and they don’t deliver like usual days?

A coffee cafe that serves toast with kaya or PB. They also do Loi Mee, curry noodles and laksa. Loi mee has the duck version too. Also minced meat noodles. Pick the type of noodle - rice or yellow.
Just limited to these noodles. Would be nice if they serve Nasi lemak too.
Coffee - typical kopitiam style or the Starbucks version. They have lots of tea selection - black tea, earl grey, green tea.  Yuzu infused, lemon etc.

A nice place to have a meal with a few friends. Very comfortable.

Serve an array of local food n of cos coffee n tea n other drinks.
Tried the curry noodle n curry with prata. Curry is Chinese style . Tasted good n will eat again.
Hot coffee is fragrant too.

Came to have breakfast, ordered kaya butter toast. Was served with this kind of egg, went back to the service crew to ask for change for soft boiled egg but the staff said that all the eggs serve today are like that, cant change.

A pretty average neighbourhood café with only 10 tables, much smaller than expected. The space felt a little cluttered, especially around the counter and cashier area.

The cake display fridge could have been cleaner, and I noticed batter and bananas stored inside as well, perhaps due to limited storage space. I’m quite particular about cleanliness when it comes to F&B, so it did affect my overall experience.

Food was decent overall. We spent about $70. The mocha wasn’t great, but everything else was good. Average, but nothing particularly memorable. Just very disturbed by the space. It definitely can be cleaner and tidier.

Also, i sat by the sliding door, and kept getting bothered by it when its slides open.

We reach quite late around 915pm and we saw their last order was at 9.30pm and the staff were cleaning the floor which looked like they about to close shop...but they actually accept our dine in order..which I have to admit, they are very kind..

We choose their recommended menu, burger meal and yuzu duck pasta which I have to admit, something new for me..

Burger meal: patty was thick and juicy..sauce was slightly spicy but delicious.

Yuzu duck pasta: first time trying yuzu (fruit based) ingredients on a pasta rather than a drink..it has a slightly sour but creamy mentaiko taste...seriously, it was a good combination with the duck breast slices..

Drinks was great too.

Althought the cost was more on the expensive side, but I would say, it was a great meal.

Do be aware that the place is very small. The first time I went here, I waited for seats but noticed that a lot of people were just being served. So I went to Swensen's instead. The second time, I got brunch with my loved one. I wanted carrot cake, but they said it was past consumption. They traded it for Lemon Meringue Pie without add-on price. Overall not a bad place.
Halal & accepts CDC.

Ambience – clean, but lacking a classy feel.
Food – average at best; it came a bit cold and didn’t really impress. Oddly, my wife’s steak arrived before my burger.
Price – $56 for two. $61 in total plus taxes. For that amount, I’d rather go to Swensen’s downstairs where the portions are bigger and the food tastes better.

Value for money? Not really, in my opinion. I’d recommend heading to Swensen’s on Level 1 instead—it’s likely cheaper too.

Friendly boss, great food and drinks!
I ordered the waffles with fried chicken. The waffles are so soft and fluffy on the inside, and crispy on the outside. The fried chicken is so juicy! Paired with the strawberry green tea that the boss recommend, it was a perfect match. Will definitely come here again!

The food was absolutely delicious and exceeded my expectations. The flavours were rich and well-balanced, and everything tasted fresh and perfectly cooked. The portion size was generous, and presentation was very appealing. Service was friendly and efficient, making the whole dining experience even more enjoyable. I would definitely come back again and highly recommend it to others!

I would like to compliment one of your staff at Swensen Dessert & Bar - Jed.

His service was truly excellent — he has a very positive attitude, is extremely helpful, cheerful, and takes great pride in his work. It is genuinely pleasant to see staff like him serving customers with such sincerity and professionalism.

He made the dining experience much more enjoyable, and I hope Swensen continues to recognise and promote staff like Jed who represent the brand so well.

Thank you for the wonderful service!

We visited the new Swensen's outlet at Plantation Plaza for a family meal. The restaurant has a nice, open layout that feels bright and welcoming.

​The Food:
Everything we ordered was good, hitting the spot for a reliable family lunch.

​Chicken Katsu Ultra Burger ($16.90): This was a highlight. The chicken cutlet was thick, very crispy on the outside, and juicy on the inside. It came topped with a generous amount of savory Katsu sauce and shredded cabbage, served on a brioche bun. It also came with a side of fries and coleslaw.

​Chicken Baked Rice ($15.90): A comfort food staple. This dish came with a good amount of melted cheese on top, covering the tender breaded chicken and savory rice in white sauce.

​Rodeo Wings ($11.90): We shared these as a side. They were crispy and glazed in a sauce that had a nice balance of sweet and spicy.

​The Dessert:
​Sticky Chewy Chocolate: You can't go to Swensen's without having ice cream. We shared this classic sundae. It is still as good as ever—rich, chocolatey, and very satisfying.

​The Drinks:
​I had an Iced Latte, my wife ordered a Cappuccino, and my son had a Coke. The coffee was a great deal as an add-on to the mains.

​Service & Verdict:
The service was a strong point—prompt, efficient, and the staff were friendly and helpful. For a total bill of $68.58 for 3 pax, it was a satisfying meal with good portions and quality service.

Had a wonderful experience at Swensen’s. The staff were very friendly, attentive, and always smiling, which made the dining experience even more enjoyable. The lady manager was especially helpful and approachable, taking the time to assist with an issue professionally and with great care. Really appreciated how attentive they were and how well they listened to customers.

The ambience was warm and welcoming, perfect for a nice meal with family and friends. The Fish & Chips & 49ers were delicious and satisfying. Highly recommended for both the food and excellent service.

I went to try their new Hazel Zero Sundae and it was delicious! The ice cream was rich and satisfying without being overly sweet. The staff member who served me was very polite and friendly, making the experience even better. I would definitely come back again!”

We enrolled our daughter in Heguru Tengah hoping to give her a fun learning experience before entering preschool, and it has truly exceeded our expectations.

Teacher Sinyi is my daughter’s favourite! 🫢 She’s naturally an introverted girl, but by around her 4th or 5th lesson, she had already warmed up and was happily interacting with her. It was so heartwarming to see. 🥰🥰

It’s amazing to see how much she’s learning while genuinely enjoying every class.

Thank you, Teacher Sinyi and Teacher Ann, for making every lesson so meaningful. 🫶🏻🫶🏻🫶🏻 We’re really grateful to be part of the Heguru Tengah family and look forward to seeing our daughter continue to grow and learn under your guidance.

Highly recommended to any parent looking for a nurturing, engaging, and enriching programme for their little one! 😊

We’ve had an excellent experience at Heguru Education Centre (Tengah), and would highly recommend both the centre and Teacher Sinyi.
We actually followed Teacher Sinyi from the HarbourFront centre to Tengah because of how impactful her teaching has been. She is exceptionally well-organised and systematic, while remaining warm, patient, and attentive to each child’s needs. She understands the children well and is able to engage them effectively, capturing their attention throughout the lesson.
What truly sets her apart is her encouraging and responsive approach. She skilfully uses questioning and scaffolding techniques to draw out children’s thinking and participation, helping them build confidence and independence in their learning.
We would recommend this centre, and in particular Teacher Sinyi, to parents looking for a structured yet supportive learning environment.

Brand new and definitely worth a visit for library lovers, those seeking some peace and quiet, and anyone interested in the history of Bukit Batok, also known as the "Coughing Hill".
Besides offering plenty of seats and reading areas, the library also features interesting sections such as SingLand Soundscape and Time Caverns.
History enthusiasts will appreciate the section showcasing the Former
Ford Factory, the very place where the British Army surrendered to the Japanese Army during World War II.
A modern and thoughtfully designed library that combines learning, history, and tranquillity, making it a wonderful place to spend a few relaxing hours.

We came across the newly reopened public library (after a renovation) located at West Mall in Bukit Batok.

The library's collection of physical books is at the 3rd floor, but it also boasts large interactive displays for readers to check out ebook recommedations in travel, health and adult fiction genres, and to borrow them on the NLB app.

One can access the library from an escalator on the first floor (there is also an entrance and exit on the 3rd floor) and an ambient sound track of birds, rustling leaves and chatter follows the visitor to the 2nd floor. Access the library proper with your SingPass (Singapore citizen).

The 2nd floor has sound installations at various sections like 'Sound Cave' and 'Time Caverns' ( which offer a history of the Bukit Batok area as well as some significant moments of Singapore's history). There is also a cosy area for visitors to read their books.

The library was quite quiet while we were there, though there was quite a number of visitors and readers. This is quite a pleasant surprise to see how our public libraries have evolved. Make a date for a visit to it, if you haven't.

Date of visit : May 2026

The Bukit Batok Public Library has reopened after renovation and now features a refreshed, modern layout. The space feels brighter and more spacious, with well-organised book sections and comfortable reading areas.
There is a good collection of books across different categories, including children’s books, fiction, and non-fiction. The environment is quiet and suitable for reading, studying, or spending some relaxed time with a book.
A great community library in the Bukit Batok area and a nice place to visit if you enjoy reading or need a calm study environment. 📚

Highly recommended! Our family last week joined the media tour of the wonderful #WestmallSG, built in 1998 & rejuvenated in 2025 🏬 Highlight was stepping into @nlbsingapore Bukit Batok Library for our first time 📖, freshly refurbished in early-2026 ✨ The SingLand Soundscape 🎼 that greeted us was unlike any library we’ve entered with its calming Sound Cave, Time Caverns, Sound Library, & Sound Pavilion 😌 Really kid friendly too!

Would appreciate if some people don't chit chat in the library. I mean.. There's many available eating places in the mall, go there. Don't chat in the library. Wanted to enjoy some peace reading the books, found a spot at the soundscape, enjoying a moment of peace and appreciating the sounds surrounding me, but there's 2 Pri. Just when I am about to enjoy some lone time on my seat, this random woman (wearing yellow top) came to squeeze in. Like hello... If I wanted company, I'll be with my friends... Why squeeze in the seat that's meant for one lol???

Tables here are quite limited too. Which is why I went to the soundscape area to sit. You have to scan the QR to reserve the table too.

Library is not spacious, I wouldn't say it is small. Just hope they set up more available tables. Nevertheless, really love the place (if people don't chit chat 😂)
